summary: Compare V3 train_vfe3.py implementation vs V2 train_vfe.py implementation (excl. coupled-fep/pure-fep) across 11 expert dimensions, then adversarially verify every high-significance claim
logs: Comparing V3 vs V2 across 11 expert dimensions; high-significance claims of ANY relationship type will be adversarially verified.
Done: 11/11 dimensions, 145 total points, 51 high verified, 10 overturned by verification.

dimensions.summary: V3 is a faithful, leaner re-implementation of V2's click-to-run + config surface: both are CLI-free "edit the dict, then run" entry points whose dataclass DEFAULTS largely coincide (kappa=1.0, lambda=1.0, e_mu_lr=0.5, e_sigma_lr=0.015, m_mu_lr=0.025, m_sigma_lr=0.0025, weight_decay=0.05), and the core free-energy weights map cleanly (V2 lambda_align -> V3 lambda_beta, both weighting the WHOLE belief-coupling block; the attention temperature tau matches via attention_tau). The big architectural shift is philosophy: V2 hard-codes valid keys via typing.Literal + literal __post_init__ lists, while V3 validates every seam against a LIVE registry (tuple(sorted(_REGISTRY))) so add-by-registering works without editing the validator. V3 collapses several V2 boolean ablation toggles into registry-key string fields (use_non_flat_transport->transport_mode, E_learnable_alpha->alpha_mode, use_positional_phi/use_rope->pos_phi/pos_rotation, irrep_spec->gauge_group+n_heads); V2 carries large v2-only surfaces (omega_direct, pure_fep/meta-agents, decode mixture, USE_FULL_COV/USE_VFE1_PRESET presets) that V3 drops, while V3 adds amp_dtype/grad_accum_steps/min_lr_frac/synthetic fallback. The click-to-run operating points differ (a tuning choice, not a structural divergence): V2 ships kappa=0.5/lambda_align=3, V3 ships kappa=1.0/lambda_beta=1.0.

points.topic: Block structure: gauge_group + n_heads vs irrep_spec
points.v3: Explicit n_heads field + gauge_group registry key: config.py:55 n_heads=8, config.py:58 gauge_group='block_glk'; click dict train_vfe3.py:78 gauge_group='block_glk', :64 n_heads=2; heads-per-block derived inside block_glk builder; embed_dim % n_heads validated config.py:322
points.v2: irrep_spec list-of-tuples + gauge_group='GLK': config.py:728 default [('l0',8,8)], train_vfe.py:29 [('fund',2,10)], :97 gauge_group='GLK'; NO n_heads field at all -- heads read as irrep_spec[0][1] (train_vfe.py:406), block dim as [0][2]; embed_dim cross-checked against sum(mult*dim) config.py:760
points.implication: Same head decomposition expressed two ways; this is the core structure knob and the strongest evidence for the pinning. V3's defaults (K=64, 8 heads) structurally equal V2's [('l0',8,8)]. V3 trades V2's per-type multi-irrep flexibility (heterogeneous (type,mult,dim) entries) for a single equal-block group selector, so genuinely heterogeneous irrep stacks are not expressible in V3.

points.topic: Belief-coupling block weight: lambda_beta vs lambda_align
points.v3: lambda_beta weights the ENTIRE belief block coupling+entropy: config.py:132 default 1.0; free_energy.py:296 F = self_total + lambda_beta*coupling, :301 + lambda_beta*entropy; click dict train_vfe3.py:118 lambda_beta=1.0
points.v2: lambda_align weights sum_beta_kl+entropy_term identically: config.py:252 default 1.0; e_step.py:903/941/1020 attn_term = lambda_align*(sum_beta_kl+entropy_term); click dict train_vfe.py:81 lambda_align=3
points.implication: Pure semantic-equivalent rename: both scale the post-softmax coupling+entropy block (NOT the energy inside the softmax), so beta=softmax(-E/tau) is unchanged and the analytic kernel stays envelope-consistent. At the matched default 1.0 the F term is byte-identical -- a core pinning anchor. The click-to-run VALUES differ (V2=3 vs V3=1.0), which is an operating-point tuning choice, not a structural difference.

points.topic: Attention temperature tau / kappa
points.v3: kappa default 1.0 (config.py:126); active tau = attention_tau(kappa, irrep_dims) = kappa*sqrt(irrep_dims[0]) (free_energy.py:32), i.e. sqrt(d_head) for block_glk; cfg.tau property is logging-only (config.py:665)
points.v2: kappa default 1.0 (config.py:254); tau = kappa*sqrt(d_head) per head under per_head_softmax=True (config.py:324, manuscript eq:per_head_temperature); banner tau computed from irrep dims
points.implication: Same canonical per-head Vaswani temperature at kappa=1. V3 routes it through one group-aware helper that also yields sqrt(K) for single-block groups (glk/so_k/sp), where V2 keys it off per_head_softmax; numerically identical for the default equal-block multihead path. Click points differ (V2 kappa=0.5 vs V3 kappa=1.0) -- tuning, not structure.

points.topic: Registry-validation philosophy vs Literal/hardcoded gates
points.v3: Every seam validated against a LIVE registry via _require(value, tuple(sorted(_REGISTRY)), name): divergence config.py:310, gauge_group :332, transport :347, alpha_mode :446, priors :438/473, norms :595, retraction :512, pos_phi :483, rope :486 -- a new registered variant is valid WITHOUT editing the validator
points.v2: typing.Literal annotations + hardcoded membership lists in __post_init__: gauge_group config.py:361/802 ('SO3','SON','GLK'), phi_preconditioner :362/1122, rope_full_gauge :965, m_lr_schedule :836, coupled_obs_mode :1387 -- adding a variant requires editing both the Literal and the runtime list
points.implication: The central modularity contract the task names. V3 realizes CLAUDE.md's add-by-registering seam discipline; V2 must touch call sites to add a group/divergence/norm. Same families are reachable today, but extensibility cost and where 'valid keys' live differ structurally. V3 documents the valid-key lists inline in the click dict comments per field; V2 scatters them across docstrings.

points.topic: Non-flat transport: transport_mode registry vs use_non_flat_transport bool
points.v3: transport_mode='flat'|'regime_ii' registry key (config.py:71) + cocycle_relaxation homotopy (config.py:76, validated [0,1] :588); click dict transport_mode='flat' train_vfe3.py:85; regime_ii edge delta_ij^a = mu_i^T W^a mu_j
points.v2: use_non_flat_transport bool (config.py:565) + non_flat_max_strength/non_flat_per_edge_delta_max (:566-567) + nonflat_delta_through_mu (:580); click dict use_non_flat_transport=False train_vfe.py:122; non-flat Omega_ij=exp(phi_i)exp(delta_ij)exp(-phi_j), delta from antisymmetric bilinear on (mu_i,mu_j)
points.implication: Both default to the flat phi-cocycle, so the ACTIVE-path numerics match (flat is byte-identical on both). The enabled non-flat formulations differ: V3's regime_ii uses a homotopy-scaled mu_i^T W^a mu_j edge factor with a single cocycle_relaxation knob; V2 uses a tanh-gated antisymmetric bilinear with two bound knobs (max_strength, per_edge_delta_max) plus a delta-through-mu grad toggle. Divergent on the opt-in path; same on default.

points.topic: Decode surface: decode_mode registry vs mixture/bias/exact-full-cov bools
points.v3: use_prior_bank gate (config.py:203) + decode_mode registry 'diagonal'|'diagonal_chunked'|'full' (config.py:205, _VALID_DECODE_MODES :21) + decode_chunk_size (:211) + decode_tau (:204); rank cross-checked vs family config.py:552
points.v2: use_prior_bank (config.py:609) + exact_full_cov_decode (:625) + decode_n_components mixture (:659) + decode_mixture_learn_weights (:665) + decode_bias (:639) + decode_tau (:466); each guarded by its own __post_init__ block (:854,:886,:926)
points.implication: Both share use_prior_bank as the KL-decode-vs-linear gate (the one sanctioned NN exception) with matching default behavior. V3 folds covariance rank into one decode_mode registry key and adds a chunked memory-win variant; V2 instead exposes separate orthogonal capability bools (mixture-of-Gaussians decode, per-vocab bias, exact full-cov). V2's mixture/bias capacity-lift toggles have NO V3 equivalent; V3's diagonal_chunked has no V2 equivalent.

points.topic: Positional encoding: pos_phi/pos_rotation registries vs use_positional_phi/use_rope bools
points.v3: pos_phi registry 'none'|'learned'|'frozen' (config.py:97) + pos_phi_compose 'bch'|'euclidean' (:98) + pos_phi_scale/project_slk (:100-101); pos_rotation registry 'none'|'rope' (:106) + rope_base/rope_full_gauge bool (:107-108)
points.v2: use_positional_phi bool (config.py:202) + bch_order (:201); use_rope bool (:433) + rope_base (:434) + rope_full_gauge tri->bi-state Literal 'off'|'both' (:435)
points.implication: Same two positional channels (phi-BCH frame + mu-RoPE). V3 promotes the on/off bools to registry strings that ALSO carry a 'frozen'/parameter-free variant (pos_phi='frozen' has no V2 analog -- V2 only has on/off). rope_full_gauge stays a bool in V3 but was a Literal string in V2; both require full covariance when enabled (V3 config.py:487, V2 :1011). Behaviorally equivalent at the default off positions.

points.topic: Adaptive self-coupling alpha: alpha_mode registry vs E_learnable_alpha bool
points.v3: alpha_mode registry 'constant'|'learnable'|'state_dependent'|'state_dependent_per_coord' (config.py:123, validated :446) + b0/c0 shape (:124-125) + alpha (:115); click dict alpha_mode='learnable' train_vfe3.py:109
points.v2: E_learnable_alpha bool (config.py:325, Bayesian alpha_i=c0/(b0+KL)) + adaptive_alpha_divergence_aware (:212) + full_cov_alpha_correction 'exact'|'heuristic' (:219) + alpha (:223); click dict E_learnable_alpha=True train_vfe.py:61
points.implication: V3 unifies the constant / state-dependent-Bayesian / learned-scalar alpha choices behind one registry key; V2 conflates two distinct mechanisms -- the state-dependent Bayesian c0/(b0+KL) form (E_learnable_alpha) and a true learned nn.Parameter -- which V3 separates into 'state_dependent' vs 'learnable'. V3 adds per-coordinate alpha (state_dependent_per_coord) gated to diagonal+renyi (config.py:453,464). The pure constant-alpha=1 default matches.

points.topic: E-step backward objective: gradient_mode + e_step_gradient vs use_autograd_mu_sigma
points.v3: gradient_mode 'filtering'|'smoothing' (config.py:196, _VALID_GRADIENT_MODES :22) selects query-side vs column-inclusive grad; SEPARATE e_step_gradient 'unroll'|'straight_through'|'detach' (:239) + detach_e_step (:240) select the backward estimator, reconciled via effective_e_step_gradient (:691)
points.v2: use_autograd_mu_sigma bool (config.py:700): False=analytic query-side 'filtering' kernel, True=autograd column-inclusive 'smoothing' grad F_red -- comment at :696 literally names 'filtering'/'smoothing'; the unroll-vs-detach distinction is the standalone amortize_sigma/two-timescale handling
points.implication: Same filtering-vs-smoothing objective distinction (V2's own comment uses the V3 names), so the mean-field-vs-total-derivative choice is preserved. V3 splits it cleanly into one knob for the objective (gradient_mode) and an orthogonal knob for the autograd estimator (e_step_gradient with straight_through as a new middle option), where V2 fused 'use autograd path' with 'compute key-side term' in a single bool.

points.topic: M-step LR groups: 3 (mu/sigma/phi) vs 6
points.v3: Three per-group LRs: m_mu_lr/m_sigma_lr/m_phi_lr (config.py:251-253, defaults .025/.0025/.015); click dict train_vfe3.py:168-170
points.v2: Six per-group LRs: m_mu_lr/m_sigma_lr/m_phi_lr PLUS m_omega_lr (config.py:92, omega_direct group), m_hyper_lr (:95, raw_c0/raw_b0/log_kappa), m_other_lr (:97, head_mixer/non_flat/output_proj); click dict train_vfe.py:147-154
points.implication: The three core belief-table LRs match by name AND default (.025/.0025/.015). V2's extra three groups exist because V2 has the parameter classes V3 dropped (omega_direct's omega_embed, a learnable-kappa/Bayesian hyper group, and a catch-all 'other' for the richer learned components). V3's leaner parameter set needs only three groups; this is a consequence of the dropped v2-only surfaces, not a different optimizer design.

points.topic: Preset override blocks: USE_FULL_COV / USE_VFE1_PRESET
points.v3: n/a - absent (no entry-script override/preset mechanism; closest is RUN_ROOT=None disabling persistence train_vfe3.py:195 and the synthetic fallback, which are unrelated)
points.v2: USE_FULL_COV opt-in block train_vfe.py:194 (config.update of diagonal_covariance/gauge_fixed_priors/use_prior_bank/exact_full_cov_decode/head-mixer-off at :342-362); USE_VFE1_PRESET train_vfe.py:208 applying presets.VFE1_OVERRIDES at :305-313, mutually exclusive (assert :308)
points.implication: V2 ships two named theoretically-pure / parity ENVELOPES as one-line toggles in the entry script -- a full-covariance pure path and a VFE_1.0-reproduction preset. V3 has no equivalent preset layer; the equivalent full-cov path in V3 must be assembled field-by-field (diagonal_covariance=False + family='gaussian_full' + decode_mode='full'). The pure full-cov capability still exists in V3, but the click-to-run convenience override does not.

points.topic: Train-stream control and dataset wiring: MAX_TOKENS/synthetic vs stride/tokenizer
points.v3: MAX_TOKENS caps train stream (train_vfe3.py:50, passed as max_tokens :252); synthetic-period3 fallback loader on FileNotFoundError (:248,:254); SEED=6 (:41), DATASET='wikitext-103' (:46), DEVICE cuda-if-available (:52)
points.v2: train_stride/eval_stride window strides (config.py:180/184, click :168/171) + eval_max_samples (:187) + TOKENIZER 'bpe'/'bytes' (train_vfe.py:233) + WIKI_JA_MAX_ARTICLES (:226); NO max-tokens cap, NO synthetic fallback (raises if cache absent); SEED=6 (:214), DATASET='wikitext-103' (:216)
points.implication: SEED, DATASET selection, and cuda-if-available DEVICE wire identically. But the corpus-shaping surface diverges: V3 caps the raw token stream (MAX_TOKENS) and guarantees a crash-free run via the deterministic period-3 synthetic stream; V2 instead controls coverage via overlapping-window stride + eval sample cap + tokenizer granularity, and will raise on a missing cache. The V3-only synthetic fallback is the train-test cutover anchor.

points.topic: Run-artifacts: RUN_ROOT + test-PPL rename
points.v3: RUN_ROOT='vfe3_runs' (train_vfe3.py:195); folder <timestamp>_<label> while training (_run_dir :269), renamed to <test_ppl:.2f>_<label> at finalize (_rename_run_by_ppl :283-316); RUN_ROOT=None disables persistence
points.v2: output_dir='vfe_runs/<prefix>_<timestamp>' (train_vfe.py:393); trainer renames to <test_ppl>=...K=..._<gauge> form at end (trainer.py:1816-1831, refreshed :501); run_prefix carries dataset/seed/steps :392
points.implication: Same artifact lifecycle: timestamped dir during training, renamed to sort by held-out test PPL at finalize. V3 drops the timestamp at rename and drops seed/steps from the label (per the branch's recent commit); V2 keeps seed/steps in the prefix. Cosmetic naming difference over the same mechanism. V3 makes the rename an entry-script helper; V2 does it inside the trainer.

points.topic: V3-only training knobs: amp_dtype, grad_accum_steps, min_lr_frac
points.v3: amp_dtype None|'bf16'|'fp16' autocast (config.py:293, validated :651); grad_accum_steps microbatch accumulation (:262, click :166); min_lr absolute floor (:265) + min_lr_frac proportional floor (:267, click train_vfe3.py:181)
points.v2: amp/grad-accum n/a - absent (no autocast or accumulation field); LR floor is m_lr_min_scale fraction-of-base (config.py:153) -- the closest analog to min_lr_frac, but no absolute min_lr
points.implication: V3 adds production/throughput knobs (mixed precision for the 5090, gradient accumulation) absent in V2, plus a two-part cosine floor (absolute min_lr AND proportional min_lr_frac) where V2 has only the proportional m_lr_min_scale. None change the pure-path math at their defaults (amp_dtype=None, grad_accum_steps=1, min_lr=0); they are off-by-default opt-ins.

points.topic: V2-only parameterization/trainer surfaces: omega_direct, pure_fep, meta-agents
points.v3: gauge_parameterization='omega_direct' is REJECTED (config.py:354 NotImplementedError -- no belief source for a non-exp GL(K) element); pure_fep / meta-agent / coupled-VFE fields absent
points.v2: Full omega_direct path (config.py:494, e_omega_lr/m_omega_lr/tie_gauge_per_type/omega_normalize_every); pure_fep backprop-free trainer block (:101-119, :1305-1358) + meta-agent fields (:158-169, K_meta/T_meta/lambda_s/tau_meta) + coupled-VFE (:228-249)
points.implication: V2 carries substantial config real estate for an alternate group-element-state parameterization and the backprop-free/meta-agent trainers; the pure_fep+meta+coupled fields feed the EXCLUDED coupled_fep/pure_fep subpackages and are peripheral here. V3 keeps only the phi-parameterization and gradient trainer, explicitly rejecting omega_direct at config time. This is the largest source of V2's ~1544-line config vs V3's ~698 lines.

verifiedHighPoints.v3: Explicit n_heads field + gauge_group registry key: config.py:55 n_heads=8, config.py:58 gauge_group='block_glk'; click dict train_vfe3.py:78 gauge_group='block_glk', :64 n_heads=2; heads-per-block derived inside block_glk builder; embed_dim % n_heads validated config.py:322
verifiedHighPoints.v2: irrep_spec list-of-tuples + gauge_group='GLK': config.py:728 default [('l0',8,8)], train_vfe.py:29 [('fund',2,10)], :97 gauge_group='GLK'; NO n_heads field at all -- heads read as irrep_spec[0][1] (train_vfe.py:406), block dim as [0][2]; embed_dim cross-checked against sum(mult*dim) config.py:760
verifiedHighPoints.implication: Same head decomposition expressed two ways; this is the core structure knob and the strongest evidence for the pinning. V3's defaults (K=64, 8 heads) structurally equal V2's [('l0',8,8)]. V3 trades V2's per-type multi-irrep flexibility (heterogeneous (type,mult,dim) entries) for a single equal-block group selector, so genuinely heterogeneous irrep stacks are not expressible in V3.

verdict.topic: Block structure: gauge_group + n_heads vs irrep_spec
verdict.evidence_v3: vfe3/geometry/generators.py:106-115 generate_glk_multihead: `for h in range(n_heads): start=h*d_head; ... G[gen_offset+idx, start+i, start+j]=1.0` then `return G.to(dtype).to(device)`; groups.py:108-116 `d_head = K // n_heads` ... `irrep_dims = [d_head] * n_heads`; config.py:322 `if self.embed_dim % self.n_heads != 0`
verdict.evidence_v2: math_utils/generators/builders.py:598-607 generate_glK_multihead_generators: `for h in range(n_heads): start=h*d_head; ... G[gen_offset+idx, start+i, start+j]=1.0` then `return G.to(dtype).to(device)`; config.py:728-730 default `[('l0',8,8)] # 8 heads x dim 8 = K=64`; config.py:1428-1430 irrep_dims `dims.extend([dim]*mult)`; config.py:1518 effective_block_dims `return self.irrep_dims`
verdict.finding: Attack failed; relationship holds as renamed. The two head-decomposition builders are byte-identical in algorithm: V3 generate_glk_multihead (generators.py:98-115) and V2 generate_glK_multihead_generators (builders.py:565-607) share the same divisibility guard, the same d_head=K//n_heads, the same n_gen_per_head=d_head*d_head, the identical row-major nested loop G[gen_offset+idx, start+i, start+j]=1.0, the same float64 assembly, and the same cast order .to(dtype).to(device). For every config expressible in BOTH repos the generators and block partition come out equal: V3 default (K=64, 8 heads) -> irrep_dims [8]*8 == V2 default [('l0',8,8)] -> effective_block_dims [8]*8; V3 click-dict (K=20, n_heads=2) -> [10]*2 == V2 train [('fund',2,10)] -> [10]*2. No missing/extra term, no sign/coefficient/eps/clamp difference, no reordered op. Citation cross-checks: V2 config.py:760 validates embed_dim==sum(mult*dim); V3 config.py:322 validates embed_dim % n_heads. The embed_dim=20 vs K=64 in the click dicts is not a discrepancy -- the claim cites the dataclass default (64) and the click override (20) separately and both repos mirror that split. One precision correction to the claim's implication (sharpens, does not flip the verdict): the lost heterogeneous-irrep flexibility does NOT live in V2's GLK path. V2's GLK builder (model.py:461-528) only handles len(irrep_spec)==1 and irrep_spec[0][1]>1 (equal multihead) and otherwise falls through to full generate_glK_generators(K) -- a genuinely multi-type spec under gauge_group='GLK' is silently collapsed to full GL(K), ignoring per-type (dim,mult) structure. Heterogeneous blocks in V2 are reachable ONLY for gauge_group='SO3'/'SON' via generate_multi_irrep_generators (builders.py:307), which emits exactly the 3 so(3) generators in spin-l (odd-dim) blocks -- an SO(N) feature where V3 offers only single-block so_k. So within the GLK gauge group the two repos are equally limited to equal blocks; the only genuine expressiveness V3 drops is SO(N) multi-irrep stacks, not GLK heterogeneity. A disclosed reduced-capability gap on a non-shared (SO-only) config is not a golden-test-breaking false-equivalence on any shared config.

verifiedHighPoints.topic: Belief-coupling block weight: lambda_beta vs lambda_align
verifiedHighPoints.v3: lambda_beta weights the ENTIRE belief block coupling+entropy: config.py:132 default 1.0; free_energy.py:296 F = self_total + lambda_beta*coupling, :301 + lambda_beta*entropy; click dict train_vfe3.py:118 lambda_beta=1.0
verifiedHighPoints.v2: lambda_align weights sum_beta_kl+entropy_term identically: config.py:252 default 1.0; e_step.py:903/941/1020 attn_term = lambda_align*(sum_beta_kl+entropy_term); click dict train_vfe.py:81 lambda_align=3
verifiedHighPoints.implication: Pure semantic-equivalent rename: both scale the post-softmax coupling+entropy block (NOT the energy inside the softmax), so beta=softmax(-E/tau) is unchanged and the analytic kernel stays envelope-consistent. At the matched default 1.0 the F term is byte-identical -- a core pinning anchor. The click-to-run VALUES differ (V2=3 vs V3=1.0), which is an operating-point tuning choice, not a structural difference.

verdict.topic: Belief-coupling block weight: lambda_beta vs lambda_align
verdict.evidence_v3: vfe3/free_energy.py:296 "F = self_total + lambda_beta * coupling" and :301 "F = F + lambda_beta * entropy"; coupling=:294 "(beta * energy).sum()", entropy=:300 "tau * (beta * (torch.log(beta...) - torch.log(pi...))).sum()", beta=:286 "attention_weights(energy, tau=tau, log_prior=log_prior)" (lambda NOT inside softmax). config.py:132 "lambda_beta: float = 1.0"; default attention_prior=:187 "causal"; attention_prior.py:59-64 causal prior B=0 for j<=i, -inf else -> softmax(B) uniform over active set. train_vfe3.py:118 "lambda_beta = 1.0".
verdict.evidence_v2: e_step.py:1014 "sum_beta_kl = (beta * kl_attn).sum()", :1016-1018 "entropy_term = tau*(beta_safe*beta_safe.log()).sum() + tau*log_N_const*beta.sum()", :1020 "attn_term = self.lambda_align * (sum_beta_kl + entropy_term) / token_count" (lambda outside softmax). core/attention.py:370 "logits = logits.masked_fill(mask == 0, float('-inf'))", :405 "beta = F.softmax(logits, dim=-1)" (pure -KL/kappa + binary mask, no log-prior bias). config.py:252 "lambda_align: float = 1.0"; train_vfe.py:81 "'lambda_align': 3".
verdict.finding: Rename holds: both scalars weight the ENTIRE post-softmax belief block (coupling sum_ij beta_ij E_ij + entropy tau sum_ij beta_ij log(beta_ij/pi_ij)) by the same factor, NOT inside the softmax, so beta=softmax(-E/tau) is unchanged in both. Default 1.0 in both; click values differ (V2=3, V3=1.0), an operating-point choice. I attacked the entropy term, the most likely place for a hidden asymmetry, and found two surface differences that are NOT behavioral: (1) V2 writes the entropy reference as +tau*log_N_const*beta.sum() (uniform pi=1/N over the causal active set, log_N_const=log(n_valid)), while V3 writes it as -tau*beta*log(pi) with pi=softmax(log_prior); V3's DEFAULT attention_prior='causal' is B=0/-inf, whose softmax is exactly uniform over the active set, so pi=1/n_valid and the two entropy forms coincide. (2) V2 injects the causal mask as a binary -inf inside the softmax while V3 adds a 0/-inf causal log-prior inside the softmax; both yield softmax over the same active set, so beta matches. The one genuine non-equivalence I can name is normalization, not structure: V2 divides attn_term (and self_term) by token_count (per-token mean of F), whereas V3's scalar free_energy returns raw sums. That is a uniform global scale shared by all F terms; it changes the reported ABSOLUTE F value but not beta, not the descent argmin, and not what lambda weights relative to the self/likelihood terms. So 'byte-identical at default 1.0' is accurate for the term-relative weighting and the softmax/entropy math, with the caveat that V3's scalar oracle is a per-token-SUM convention vs V2's per-token-MEAN. No missing/extra term, no sign/coefficient/power flip, no different clamp/eps that alters semantics (V3 log_eps clamp on beta/pi for numerical safety; V2 beta_safe clamp at _BETA_LOG_FLOOR — both numerical floors, not semantic). The 'renamed' relationship survives the adversarial attack.

verifiedHighPoints.topic: Attention temperature tau / kappa
verifiedHighPoints.v3: kappa default 1.0 (config.py:126); active tau = attention_tau(kappa, irrep_dims) = kappa*sqrt(irrep_dims[0]) (free_energy.py:32), i.e. sqrt(d_head) for block_glk; cfg.tau property is logging-only (config.py:665)
verifiedHighPoints.v2: kappa default 1.0 (config.py:254); tau = kappa*sqrt(d_head) per head under per_head_softmax=True (config.py:324, manuscript eq:per_head_temperature); banner tau computed from irrep dims
verifiedHighPoints.implication: Same canonical per-head Vaswani temperature at kappa=1. V3 routes it through one group-aware helper that also yields sqrt(K) for single-block groups (glk/so_k/sp), where V2 keys it off per_head_softmax; numerically identical for the default equal-block multihead path. Click points differ (V2 kappa=0.5 vs V3 kappa=1.0) -- tuning, not structure.

verdict.topic: Attention temperature tau / kappa
verdict.evidence_v3: vfe3/free_energy.py:32 "return kappa * (irrep_dims[0] ** 0.5)" (active tau via attention_tau, called as model.py:683 "tau=attention_tau(cfg.kappa, self.group.irrep_dims)"); config.py:126 "kappa: float = 1.0"; config.py:665 cfg.tau property "return self.kappa * (self.d_head ** 0.5)" is logging-only
verdict.evidence_v2: config.py:254 "kappa: float = 1.0", config.py:324 "per_head_softmax: bool = True"; default per-head path e_step.py:1257-1267 calls fused kernel with "mu_q=mu_h ... irrep_dims=[d_h]" per head; kernel core/vfe_gradients.py:231 "B, N, K = mu_q.shape" then :58/:671 "_kappa_scaled: return max(kappa * math.sqrt(max(K, 1)), eps)" -> K=d_h so tau_h = kappa*sqrt(d_head)
verdict.finding: Verified by reading the actual computation in both repos, not comments. V3 active tau = kappa*irrep_dims[0]**0.5; for the default block_glk (irrep_dims=[d_head]*H) this is kappa*sqrt(d_head), applied as a single scalar to the per-head energy (H,N,N) whose softmax over the last axis is genuinely per-head. V2's default per_head_softmax=True loop feeds each head's d_head-wide slice to the fused kernel where B,N,K=mu_q.shape makes K=d_h, so kappa_scaled=kappa*sqrt(d_head) per head. With equal blocks and the default shared scalar kappa, every head's tau equals kappa*sqrt(d_head) in both. At kappa=1 these are numerically identical on the default equal-block multihead path; single-block groups (glk/so_k/sp) give kappa*sqrt(K) in both (V3 via irrep_dims=[K]; V2 via the single-softmax fused/non_flat.py:565 path). The relationship survives. Two genuine but OFF-DEFAULT structural asymmetries hide under the equivalence, neither breaking identity at the compared operating point: (1) V2's _kappa_scaled clamps max(.,eps) (core/vfe_gradients.py:58); V3's attention_tau has NO eps floor -- inert at kappa=1, d_head>=1 (value >> eps~1e-6), would diverge only as kappa->0. (2) V2 can route a LEARNABLE per-head kappa vector log_kappa_per_head (kappa_h_eff=_kappa[h], config.py:330-337, e_step.py:1253-1256) giving heads distinct temperatures; V3's single scalar tau cannot express per-head kappa -- but both DEFAULTS use one shared scalar kappa, so the default path is identical. Note the click-point kappa=0.5 cited for V2 lives in train_pure_fep.py:93 (pure_fep, excluded scope); the in-scope V2 dataclass default is config.py:254=1.0, matching V3's 1.0, so the implication's claimed kappa=0.5 vs 1.0 click-point gap does not apply to the in-scope entry config (both 1.0). This strengthens, not weakens, the "same" verdict at default. Could not run advisor (rate-limited); verdict rests on direct code reads in both repos.

verifiedHighPoints.topic: Registry-validation philosophy vs Literal/hardcoded gates
verifiedHighPoints.v3: Every seam validated against a LIVE registry via _require(value, tuple(sorted(_REGISTRY)), name): divergence config.py:310, gauge_group :332, transport :347, alpha_mode :446, priors :438/473, norms :595, retraction :512, pos_phi :483, rope :486 -- a new registered variant is valid WITHOUT editing the validator
verifiedHighPoints.v2: typing.Literal annotations + hardcoded membership lists in __post_init__: gauge_group config.py:361/802 ('SO3','SON','GLK'), phi_preconditioner :362/1122, rope_full_gauge :965, m_lr_schedule :836, coupled_obs_mode :1387 -- adding a variant requires editing both the Literal and the runtime list
verifiedHighPoints.implication: The central modularity contract the task names. V3 realizes CLAUDE.md's add-by-registering seam discipline; V2 must touch call sites to add a group/divergence/norm. Same families are reachable today, but extensibility cost and where 'valid keys' live differ structurally. V3 documents the valid-key lists inline in the click dict comments per field; V2 scatters them across docstrings.

verdict.topic: Registry-validation philosophy vs Literal/hardcoded gates
verdict.evidence_v3: vfe3/config.py:332 `_require(self.gauge_group, tuple(sorted(_GROUPS)), "gauge_group")`; :310 divergence, :347 transport, :446 alpha_mode, :438/:473 priors, :595 norms, :512 retraction, :483 pos_phi, :486 rope — all `_require(value, tuple(sorted(_REGISTRY)), name)`. groups.py:57 `def register_group(name)` -> `_GROUPS[name]=fn`; identical `register_*` decorators exist for transport, retraction, norms, alpha_i, attention_prior, rope, positional_phi.
verdict.evidence_v2: config.py:361 `gauge_group: Literal['SO3', 'SON', 'GLK'] = 'GLK'`; :802 `if self.gauge_group not in ('SO3', 'SON', 'GLK')`; :1122 `if self.phi_preconditioner not in ('clip', 'killing', 'killing_per_block')`; :836 `if self.m_lr_schedule not in ('constant', 'linear', 'cosine')`; :1387 `if self.coupled_obs_mode not in ("honest", "leak", "filter")`; :1442 `n_gen` dispatch `if self.gauge_group == 'SO3' ... elif 'GLK' ... else SON`. Zero `tuple(sorted(...))`/`_REGISTRY` matches in the whole file.
verdict.finding: The divergent relationship survives the attack. Tried to refute three ways. (1) Does V2 also validate against a live registry somewhere? No — config.py has zero registry-style validation (0 hits for `tuple(sorted(`, `_REGISTRY`, `register`); 20 hits for `not in (...)`/`Literal[`). (2) Does V3's claimed registry actually exist live? Yes — every cited seam imports a real `_REGISTRY` dict populated by a `@register_*` decorator (groups.py:57, transport.py:92, retraction.py:24, norms.py:16, alpha_i.py:19, attention_prior.py:20, rope.py:18, positional_phi.py:22), and `_require` checks membership in `tuple(sorted(_REGISTRY))`, so a newly registered variant is config-valid with no validator edit. V2 by contrast forces three coordinated edits to add a group: the `Literal` annotation (:361), the runtime tuple (:802), and the hardcoded `n_gen` if/elif dispatch (:1442). (3) The one genuine imprecision: the claim says V3 validates EVERY seam against a registry, but V3 still keeps hardcoded literal tuples for several NON-cited seams (`_VALID_GAUGE_PARAM` :19, `_VALID_PHI_PRECOND_MODES` :23, `_VALID_PHI_RETRACT_MODES` :24, `_VALID_GRADIENT_MODES` :22, `_VALID_POS_PHI_COMPOSE` :25, `_VALID_PRIOR_SOURCES` :26, `_VALID_E_STEP_GRADIENTS` :27) — so "Every seam" is overstated. However, every seam the claim SPECIFICALLY cites is genuinely registry-backed, and V2 has none, so the overstatement does not flip the structural difference. Relationship is divergent as claimed; the only correction is to qualify "Every" to "every cited seam."

dimensions.summary: V3's train.py is a deliberate, leaner reimplementation of V2's VFETrainer M-step, not a line-for-line port. The core mechanics match: AdamW with per-group LRs over the prior tables, a LambdaLR warmup-then-cosine schedule, external global-norm grad clipping, token-weighted CE evaluation, best-by-val-PPL checkpointing, and a rename-run-dir-by-test-PPL convention. But V3 collapses V2's seven-to-eight param groups (mu/sigma/phi/omega/hyper/nonflat/decode/other) into three LRs (mu/sigma/phi), drops V2's RiemannianAdamW geometric optimizer path entirely, changes the warmup formula (V3 wastes step 0 at LR=0; V2 does not), uses a different two-floor LR scheme, and adds grad accumulation that V2 lacks. Neither side uses AMP/autocast/GradScaler, and neither trainer.py has a FLOPs banner. The most consequential divergence is that V3 has no Riemannian/Killing-preconditioned gauge optimizer, so phi/omega updates are plain Euclidean AdamW rather than natural-gradient — a genuine optimizer-dynamics difference, not a refactor.

points.topic: Per-group LR count: 3 (mu/sigma/phi) vs up to 8 named groups
points.v3: build_optimizer creates 3 base groups keyed by m_mu_lr/m_sigma_lr/m_phi_lr (train.py:67-71); optional toggled params reuse those same three LRs (output_proj@m_mu, mixer@m_mu, pos_phi@m_phi, s_mu@m_mu, s_sigma@m_sigma, connection_W@m_phi, log_alpha@m_mu, log_lambda_beta@m_phi; train.py:72-90). Config has ONLY m_mu_lr/m_sigma_lr/m_phi_lr (config.py:251-253) — no m_omega_lr/m_hyper_lr/m_other_lr.
points.v2: _build_optimizer routes named_parameters by substring into 8 groups with 6 distinct LRs: m_mu(m_mu_lr), m_sigma(m_sigma_lr), m_phi(m_phi_lr), m_hyper(m_hyper_lr), m_nonflat(m_other_lr), m_decode(m_other_lr), m_other(m_other_lr), omega_embed(m_omega_lr) (trainer.py:379-409).
points.implication: V3 gives hyper/temperature/decode-bias/connection params one of the three belief LRs rather than dedicated m_hyper_lr/m_other_lr/m_omega_lr knobs. Where V2 tuned omega at m_omega_lr and hypers at m_hyper_lr, V3 cannot independently set those rates — any V2 run that relied on m_omega_lr != m_phi_lr or m_hyper_lr != m_sigma_lr is not reproducible in V3. Defensible since V3's model surface is smaller (no separate omega_embed/raw_c0/raw_b0 tables), but it is a real loss of optimizer granularity.

points.topic: RiemannianAdamW (Killing-preconditioned, GL+(K)-retracted) gauge optimizer
points.v3: n/a - absent. build_optimizer always returns torch.optim.AdamW (train.py:117); phi_embed/connection_W are updated by plain Euclidean AdamW. Only choice is fused-vs-standard AdamW (train.py:116).
points.v2: Under gauge_parameterization in ('phi','omega_direct') with a generators buffer, _build_optimizer returns RiemannianAdamW with a per-block or ambient Killing-form preconditioner, omega retraction onto GL+(K) via _omega_step, and phi Killing-preconditioning (trainer.py:414-462). Plain AdamW is only the fallback.
points.implication: This is the single largest optimizer-DYNAMICS divergence. V2's phi/omega gradients can be taken as natural gradients in the Killing metric and omega is geometrically retracted to stay on GL+(K); V3 takes raw Euclidean steps on phi coordinates with no retraction or metric preconditioning. The trajectories through parameter space differ even with identical LRs/losses; the golden-test pinning is at the kernel/F-value level, not the optimizer-step level, so this is not pinned.

points.topic: Warmup formula: step/warmup (LR=0 at step 0) vs (step+1)/warmup (no wasted step)
points.v3: lr_lambda returns step / max(1, warmup_steps) during warmup (train.py:134-135). At step 0 this is 0.0 — the first optimizer step has zero LR (a wasted update).
points.v2: lr_lambda returns (step + 1) / max(warmup_steps, 1) (trainer.py:490), with an explicit comment that step+1 avoids leaving training step 0 at LR=0; it hits 1.0 at step==warmup-1 for a continuous handoff to the decay branch.
points.implication: Genuine numerical difference in the first warmup_steps. V3's effective warmup is one step shorter in delivered LR and the very first AdamW step does nothing (only advances Adam's bias-correction counter on a zero grad-scaled update — actually a no-op move). Over a 15000-step run the effect is tiny, but it is a real, intentional-in-V2 / dropped-in-V3 behavior, and it means step-by-step LR schedules are not byte-identical.

points.topic: LR floor scheme: per-group abs+frac floor (min_lr, min_lr_frac) vs single multiplier floor (m_lr_min_scale)
points.v3: _floor_lr_lambdas builds a per-group multiplier floor = max(min_lr/base, min_lr_frac), giving absolute LR floor max(min_lr, min_lr_frac*base, base*cosine) (train.py:140-163). TWO floors: shared absolute min_lr and per-group-base-proportional min_lr_frac. A base-0 group stays frozen. Defaults min_lr=1e-4, min_lr_frac=0.0 (config.py:265-268).
points.v2: _build_scheduler uses one floor = m_lr_min_scale (a FRACTION of base), applied uniformly to the cosine shape: floor + (1-floor)*shape (trainer.py:481,498). Also supports schedule in {cosine,linear,constant} (trainer.py:482,492-497).
points.implication: Different floor algebra. V2's floor is purely fractional (per-group base scales with it automatically) and folds into the cosine as floor+(1-floor)*cos; V3 adds an ABSOLUTE shared floor min_lr and applies max(floor, cosine) (a hard clamp, not a convex blend). The shapes near the tail differ: V2 never lets the term below floor*base via blending; V3 clamps the multiplier. Also V3 dropped V2's linear/constant schedule options — V3 is cosine-only.

points.topic: Gradient accumulation (microbatch chunking)
points.v3: train_step splits the batch into grad_accum_steps chunks along dim 0, divides each microbatch loss by K and backward-accumulates, with one clip+step+scheduler.step at the boundary; K==1 is the byte-identical single-backward path (train.py:222-244). cfg.grad_accum_steps default 1 (config.py:262), validated >=1 (config.py:639-640).
points.v2: n/a - absent. train_step does a single forward/backward on the whole batch (trainer.py:1335-1339); no chunking, no accumulation knob.
points.implication: V3 adds an effective-batch-size lever V2 lacks. With equal-token microbatches (B % K == 0, no per-position ignore reweighting) the accumulated grad equals the full-batch grad to round-off, so at K=1 it is identical to V2's single backward; for K>1 it changes memory/throughput, not the math. A net capability addition, not a divergence in the default path.

points.topic: AMP / autocast / GradScaler / mixed-precision
points.v3: n/a - absent. train_step runs a plain loss.backward() with no autocast context and no scaler (train.py:223-242). Project mandates float32 throughout.
points.v2: n/a - absent. train_step does plain loss.backward() (trainer.py:1339); a file-wide grep for autocast/GradScaler/scaler/bfloat16/float16 finds no AMP machinery in trainer.py.
points.implication: Both train in full float32 with no mixed precision. The task's AMP-dtype question resolves to: neither side has it. Equivalent, and consistent with the CLAUDE.md float32 hard constraint.

points.topic: Gradient clipping: external global-norm clip before step
points.v3: clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), grad_clip) when grad_clip>0, applied once after accumulation, before optimizer.step (train.py:240-242); default grad_clip=1.0 (train.py:198,301).
points.v2: clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), cfg.grad_clip) when cfg.grad_clip>0 AND not riemannian_internal_clip, before optimizer.step (trainer.py:1358-1361).
points.implication: Same external Euclidean global-norm clip at the same point in the step. V2 additionally has a riemannian_internal_clip mode that defers the clip to AFTER Killing preconditioning inside RiemannianAdamW (trainer.py:456-458); V3 has only the external clip (it has no Riemannian path to defer into). For the pure/default path the clip behavior matches.

points.topic: Weight-decay grouping and gauge-protection (phi/omega wd=0)
points.v3: Single shared cfg.weight_decay passed to AdamW (train.py:117); ALL groups including phi_embed inherit it — no per-group weight_decay=0 protection for the gauge frame. cfg.weight_decay default 0.05 (config.py:254).
points.v2: phi group pinned weight_decay=0 by default (gauge protection: decaying phi->0 biases Omega toward identity), omega_embed/m_hyper/m_nonflat/m_decode all weight_decay=0, with opt-in embed/non_embed_weight_decay overrides (trainer.py:377,386,390,393,397,400-409).
points.implication: Genuine difference: V2 deliberately shields gauge-frame and prior-bias params from L2 decay; V3 applies the one shared weight_decay to phi_embed too. With nonzero weight_decay (V3 default 0.05) this biases V3's learned gauge coordinates toward zero (transport toward identity) in a way V2 default explicitly prevents. The pure path exists only at weight_decay=0; V3 has no per-group decay-zero pin. Worth flagging as a behavioral drift under the default config.

points.topic: Coverage guard: assert every trainable param is grouped
points.v3: After grouping, build_optimizer computes missing = {trainable params} - grouped and raises AssertionError if any param would be left ungrouped/untrained (train.py:106-112); frozen (requires_grad=False) params like the hyper-prior centroid r are exempt.
points.v2: n/a - absent. _build_optimizer routes by substring with an m_other catch-all (trainer.py:339-340) and filters empty groups (trainer.py:412), but has no assertion that the union covers all parameters — a new param simply falls into m_other.
points.implication: V3 trades V2's catch-all m_other group for an explicit exact-coverage guard. Safer against silently-frozen new params, but means a newly added trainable param HARD-FAILS in V3 (must be explicitly grouped) where V2 would silently train it at m_other_lr. Different failure mode, both defensible.

points.topic: train()/evaluate() free functions vs VFETrainer class
points.v3: train(), evaluate(), train_step(), build_optimizer(), lr_lambda() are module-level free functions taking explicit args; optimizer/scheduler are built inside train() and threaded through train_step (train.py:37,189,247,294). State (artifacts, last_val) is local to train().
points.v2: VFETrainer class (trainer.py:92) holds self.model/optimizer/scheduler/global_step/output_dir/_metrics_tracker/_pub_tracker as instance state; train_step/evaluate/train are methods mutating self.global_step etc.
points.implication: Pure structural refactor — same computation, different packaging. V3's free-function design with an opt-in RunArtifacts object replaces V2's stateful trainer + _metrics_tracker/_pub_tracker. The silent path (artifacts=None) is bit-clean in V3; V2 always carries the tracker state. No numerical consequence.

points.topic: Token-weighted CE evaluation and BPC units
points.v3: evaluate accumulates float(ce)*n_b weighted by non-ignored token count n_b, returns ce, ppl=exp(min(ce,20)), bpc=ce/ln2 (bits per TOKEN) (train.py:247-291); cap is eval_max_batches (config.py:277).
points.v2: evaluate accumulates ce_for_log * n_tokens on-device, avg_ce = total_ce/total_tokens, val_ppl=exp(min(avg_ce,20)); but val_bpc is char-corrected via bpc_from_dataset/tokens_per_char (trainer.py:1465-1494); cap is eval_max_samples (window cap).
points.implication: CE and PPL are computed identically (token-weighted mean CE, same PPL clamp at 20). The BPC differs: V2 reports bits-per-CHARACTER (corrected by a tokens_per_char sidecar) so periodic val and final test BPC are comparable in chars; V3 reports bits-per-TOKEN (ce/ln2). The eval cap also changed units: V2 caps by samples/windows, V3 by batches. PPL/CE comparable; BPC numbers are NOT directly comparable across the two.

points.topic: Best-model + checkpoint cadence + rename-run-dir-by-test-PPL
points.v3: maybe_save_best saves state_dict at new-min val PPL (run_artifacts.py:91-98); save_checkpoint writes step/model/optimizer/config (run_artifacts.py:145-160) at cfg.checkpoint_interval (train.py:474-476); finalize_run reloads best, scores test, then _rename_run_by_ppl -> <test_ppl:.2f>_<label> with _N clash suffix, OSError-swallowed (train_vfe3.py:283-316).
points.v2: Saves best_model.pt at new-min val_loss (trainer.py:2158-2162); _save_checkpoint at checkpoint_interval (trainer.py:2165-2166); end-of-train reloads best, run_test_evaluation, then renames output_dir -> <test_ppl:.2f>=test-PPL_K=<K>_<gauge_label> with HHMMSS clash suffix, OSError-swallowed (trainer.py:2192-2237).
points.implication: Functionally equivalent early-stopping + rename convention: both reload the BEST-val checkpoint before the headline test eval, both encode test PPL into the folder name, both swallow rename I/O errors. Differences are cosmetic: V3 selects best by val PPL vs V2 by val loss (monotone-equivalent for PPL=exp(CE)); name format (V3 <ppl>_<dataset_K_group> vs V2 <ppl>=test-PPL_K=<K>_<gauge_label>); clash suffix (_2/_3 vs _HHMMSS). The rename lives in train_vfe3.py for V3, not run_artifacts.py.

points.topic: Init banner; FLOPs counter
points.v3: _banner prints a 64-char-bar VFE_2.0-style header (params, device, K/N/L/heads/group/family, steps/batch/dataset, M-LRs, alpha/kappa/tau/mass_phi, seed) in BOTH train.py:480-496 and train_vfe3.py:212-227; docstring explicitly says 'no FLOPs counter' (train.py:481).
points.v2: No banner block; train() logs a single line 'Starting training: N steps, P params, device=...' (trainer.py:1956-1957). A file-wide grep for FLOP/flops/MFU/banner in trainer.py finds NOTHING.
points.implication: Contrary to the task premise, V2's trainer.py has NO FLOPs banner (and no multi-line banner at all) — if one exists it is in another V2 module out of scope here. V3 actually ADDED the structured multi-line banner. Both sides lack a FLOPs/MFU counter. So on FLOPs banner specifically: equivalent (absent in both); on init banner: V3 is richer, a v3-side addition.

verifiedHighPoints.topic: Per-group LR count: 3 (mu/sigma/phi) vs up to 8 named groups
verifiedHighPoints.v3: build_optimizer creates 3 base groups keyed by m_mu_lr/m_sigma_lr/m_phi_lr (train.py:67-71); optional toggled params reuse those same three LRs (output_proj@m_mu, mixer@m_mu, pos_phi@m_phi, s_mu@m_mu, s_sigma@m_sigma, connection_W@m_phi, log_alpha@m_mu, log_lambda_beta@m_phi; train.py:72-90). Config has ONLY m_mu_lr/m_sigma_lr/m_phi_lr (config.py:251-253) — no m_omega_lr/m_hyper_lr/m_other_lr.
verifiedHighPoints.v2: _build_optimizer routes named_parameters by substring into 8 groups with 6 distinct LRs: m_mu(m_mu_lr), m_sigma(m_sigma_lr), m_phi(m_phi_lr), m_hyper(m_hyper_lr), m_nonflat(m_other_lr), m_decode(m_other_lr), m_other(m_other_lr), omega_embed(m_omega_lr) (trainer.py:379-409).
verifiedHighPoints.implication: V3 gives hyper/temperature/decode-bias/connection params one of the three belief LRs rather than dedicated m_hyper_lr/m_other_lr/m_omega_lr knobs. Where V2 tuned omega at m_omega_lr and hypers at m_hyper_lr, V3 cannot independently set those rates — any V2 run that relied on m_omega_lr != m_phi_lr or m_hyper_lr != m_sigma_lr is not reproducible in V3. Defensible since V3's model surface is smaller (no separate omega_embed/raw_c0/raw_b0 tables), but it is a real loss of optimizer granularity.

verdict.topic: Per-group LR count: 3 (mu/sigma/phi) vs up to 8 named groups
verdict.evidence_v3: vfe3/train.py:67-71 builds exactly 3 base groups: {"params":[pb.mu_embed],"lr":cfg.m_mu_lr}, {"params":[pb.sigma_log_embed, pb.decode_log_scale],"lr":cfg.m_sigma_lr}, {"params":[pb.phi_embed],"lr":cfg.m_phi_lr}. Toggled params reuse those 3 LRs (train.py:73 output_proj@m_mu_lr, :75 head_mixer@m_mu_lr, :77 pos_phi_free@m_phi_lr, :79 s_mu_embed@m_mu_lr, :80 s_sigma_log_embed@m_sigma_lr, :86 connection_W@m_phi_lr, :88 log_alpha@m_mu_lr, :90 log_lambda_beta@m_phi_lr). vfe3/config.py:251-253 defines ONLY m_mu_lr/m_sigma_lr/m_phi_lr; a repo-wide grep for m_omega_lr/m_hyper_lr/m_other_lr hits only docs/comparisons/_raw_workflow_output_2026-06-07.json (a doc artifact), never code.
verdict.evidence_v2: transformer/vfe/trainer.py:379-409 builds 8 named groups: m_mu(cfg.m_mu_lr), m_sigma/m_logvar(cfg.m_sigma_lr), m_phi(cfg.m_phi_lr), m_hyper(cfg.m_hyper_lr), m_nonflat(cfg.m_other_lr), m_decode(cfg.m_other_lr), m_other(cfg.m_other_lr), and conditionally omega_embed(cfg.m_omega_lr) at :405. config.py:89-97 sets SIX distinct knobs with distinct defaults: m_mu_lr=0.025, m_sigma_lr=0.0025, m_phi_lr=0.015, m_omega_lr=0.015, m_hyper_lr=0.0125, m_other_lr=0.02.
verdict.finding: The divergence holds under attack. Three independent refutation attempts failed: (1) The 'missing' V3 symbols m_omega_lr/m_hyper_lr/m_other_lr exist nowhere in V3 code — grep finds them only inside a comparison-output JSON doc, so there is no renamed/relocated equivalent. (2) V2's distinct LRs do not collapse to belief-LR values by default: m_hyper_lr=0.0125 and m_other_lr=0.02 are genuinely distinct from m_mu_lr/m_sigma_lr/m_phi_lr, so V2 really can tune hypers and 'other' independently. (Sole near-coincidence: m_omega_lr=0.015=m_phi_lr by default, but it stays a separate, independently settable knob.) (3) Every one of the 8 toggled-param LR mappings the comparing agent cited for V3 is exactly correct against train.py:73-90, including log_lambda_beta@m_phi_lr. One nuance not in the claim but consistent with it: V2 maps decode_log_scale to m_hyper_lr (trainer.py:319-329), whereas V3 maps decode_log_scale to m_sigma_lr (train.py:69) — a further sub-divergence reinforcing, not weakening, the claim. Conclusion: V3 collapses V2's six-LR / eight-group optimizer surface to three LRs; any V2 run relying on m_hyper_lr!=m_sigma_lr or m_other_lr!=a-belief-LR (true at defaults) is not reproducible in V3. relationship=divergent, significance high, confirmed.

verifiedHighPoints.topic: RiemannianAdamW (Killing-preconditioned, GL+(K)-retracted) gauge optimizer
verifiedHighPoints.v3: n/a - absent. build_optimizer always returns torch.optim.AdamW (train.py:117); phi_embed/connection_W are updated by plain Euclidean AdamW. Only choice is fused-vs-standard AdamW (train.py:116).
verifiedHighPoints.v2: Under gauge_parameterization in ('phi','omega_direct') with a generators buffer, _build_optimizer returns RiemannianAdamW with a per-block or ambient Killing-form preconditioner, omega retraction onto GL+(K) via _omega_step, and phi Killing-preconditioning (trainer.py:414-462). Plain AdamW is only the fallback.
verifiedHighPoints.implication: This is the single largest optimizer-DYNAMICS divergence. V2's phi/omega gradients can be taken as natural gradients in the Killing metric and omega is geometrically retracted to stay on GL+(K); V3 takes raw Euclidean steps on phi coordinates with no retraction or metric preconditioning. The trajectories through parameter space differ even with identical LRs/losses; the golden-test pinning is at the kernel/F-value level, not the optimizer-step level, so this is not pinned.

verdict.topic: RiemannianAdamW (Killing-preconditioned, GL+(K)-retracted) gauge optimizer
verdict.evidence_v3: vfe3/train.py:117 "return torch.optim.AdamW(groups, weight_decay=cfg.weight_decay, fused=use_fused)" — build_optimizer's only return; phi_embed (line 70) and connection_W (line 86) are plain AdamW param groups. No RiemannianAdamW class exists in V3 source (the literal string occurs ONLY in docs/comparisons/*.json artifacts). V3's Killing preconditioner (phi_preconditioner.py: build_killing_preconditioner, _precond_killing) and retract_phi are wired into the E-STEP, not the M-step: e_step.py:399-402 "grad_phi = precondition_phi_gradient(grad_phi, belief.phi, ...)" then "phi = retract_phi(belief.phi, -grad_phi, ...)" — operating on the per-iteration BELIEF phi, not the prior-table phi_embed.
verdict.evidence_v2: transformer/training/optimizer.py:36 "class RiemannianAdamW(torch.optim.AdamW):"; line 215 _precondition_phi "grad <- grad @ killing_inv"; line 390-393 _omega_step "retraction that keeps each matrix in GL^+(K)" with line 489 "group-exponential retraction Omega_new = Omega . exp(-lr . step_mat)". Selected as the M-step optimizer in trainer.py:445 "return RiemannianAdamW(param_groups, killing_inv=killing_inv, ...)" under use_riemannian = cfg.gauge_parameterization in ('phi','omega_direct') and generators is not None (trainer.py:346-349); plain torch.optim.AdamW is only the fallback (trainer.py:464).
verdict.finding: The relationship survives the attack. V2 genuinely has a Killing-preconditioned, GL+(K)-retracted M-step optimizer (RiemannianAdamW) selected for gauge parameterizations; V3's build_optimizer unconditionally returns torch.optim.AdamW (only fused-vs-standard differs), taking raw Euclidean steps on phi_embed/connection_W with no metric preconditioning or retraction. The one subtlety a refutation could exploit is that V3 DOES contain Killing-preconditioner and retract_phi machinery (vfe3/geometry/phi_preconditioner.py, retraction.py) — but this is the wrong phase: it preconditions/retracts the per-iteration BELIEF phi inside the unrolled E-step (inference; e_step.py:399-402, operating on belief.phi), not the prior-table gauge parameter phi_embed updated by the M-step optimizer. So the optimizer-DYNAMICS divergence is real and not pinned by the kernel/F-value golden tests. Two minor citation defects in the original claim, neither changing the verdict: (1) the V3 path should be vfe3/train.py, not bare train.py (the line numbers 116/117 match exactly); (2) the implication slightly overstates by implying V3 has NO retraction/preconditioning at all — it has both, but only in the E-step, never in the M-step. The high significance is justified.

dimensions.dimension: Model forward / block / stack — the encode -> E-step -> decode pipeline, per-block/per-stack structure, belief handoff, normalization, head/irrep threading, and gauge frame phi placement.
dimensions.summary: V3 and V2 share the same skeletal pipeline (encode -> positional phi composition -> L blocks of unrolled E-step -> final norm -> KL/linear decode -> CE) and the same cross-block handoff recurrence for the mean, so at the default n_layers=1 the two are structurally congruent up to refactor. The genuine divergences are: (1) where the head mixer runs — V2 applies it per block (L times, interleaved with the E-step), V3 applies it once after the whole stack; (2) the sigma handoff reference and SPD repair — V2 blends posterior sigma against the FROZEN embedding sigma with a full-cov eigenvalue-floor/symmetrize branch, V3 blends against the RUNNING prior sigma with a plain convex combination; (3) the outer/M-step objective — V2 routes hyperparameter grads through per-block detached `_aux_hyperparam_loss` scalars, V3 has no aux loss and instead threads learned params through the unrolled E-step plus explicit `mstep_self_coupling`/`lambda_h`/`gamma_coupling` loss terms. V3 also vectorizes the E-step over the batch, encodes attention causality as an additive 0/-inf log-prior (vs V2's multiplicative tril mask), and adds a fused-chunked decode + AMP fp32 island absent in V2. The mean-handoff core that must match V2 numerically is preserved.

points.topic: Top-level forward pipeline (encode -> positional phi -> stack -> final norm -> decode -> CE)
points.v3: model.py:312-413 forward(): prior_bank.encode -> _apply_pos_phi -> vfe_stack -> head_mixer -> final_norm -> prior_bank.decode -> F.cross_entropy
points.v2: model.py:143-238 _encode_step_decode(): prior_bank.encode -> pos_enc -> stack -> final_norm -> prior_bank.decode/output_proj; CE in forward() model.py:295-348
points.implication: The macro architecture is identical up to refactor: both are parameter-free-block stacks whose only learnable capacity is the PriorBank, with the E-step unrolled into the training graph so CE backprops through inference. This is the equivalence the golden tests pin; it must match for V3 to be a faithful reimplementation.

points.topic: Cross-block MEAN handoff mu_q -> mu_p recurrence
points.v3: stack.py:47,54 rho=cfg.prior_handoff_rho; mu_p = (1.0-rho)*mu_p + rho*belief.mu each layer (always the blend form)
points.v2: stack.py:121-126 rho_mu=prior_handoff_rho; at rho==1.0 new_prior_mu=beliefs.mu (alias), else (1-rho)*priors.mu + rho*beliefs.mu
points.implication: Same recurrence: both blend the running prior mean against the just-converged belief mean. At the default rho=1.0 V2 takes the aliasing fast path (new_prior_mu=beliefs.mu) and V3 computes 0*mu_p+1*belief.mu; numerically identical. Defaults match (rho=1.0). This is the core belief-to-prior cascade that must agree with V2.

points.topic: Cross-block SIGMA handoff reference + SPD repair
points.v3: stack.py:55 sigma_p = (1.0-rho_s)*sigma_p + rho_s*belief.sigma — blends against the RUNNING sigma_p, plain convex combination, no clamp/eigenvalue-floor
points.v2: stack.py:130-165 blends against FROZEN initial_priors.sigma; rho=0 frozen branch clamps [1e-4,sigma_max]; rho>0 full-cov branch does symmetrize + safe_eigh_backward eigenvalue floor [1e-4,sigma_max] + re-symmetrize
points.implication: Two real differences. (a) Reference: V2 always references the FIXED embedding sigma; V3 references the running sigma_p, so the recurrences differ once n_layers>=3 with rho_sigma>0 (identical at depth<=2 / one handoff, and identical at the rho_sigma=0 default since both reduce to the embedding sigma). (b) V2 applies an SPD eigenvalue-floor/symmetrize for full-cov sigma that V3 lacks; V3 defaults diagonal_covariance=True (config.py:111) so the stack blend is diagonal (N,K) and the convex combination stays positive without repair — the SPD branch is effectively v2_only for the full-cov ablation. Dormant at the rho_sigma=0 default for both.

points.topic: Head mixer application frequency: per-block (V2) vs once-after-stack (V3)
points.v3: model.py:96 head_mixer built once; model.py:364-365 applied ONCE after vfe_stack, before final_norm
points.v2: block.py:153-156 mixer built per VFEBlock; block.py:238-240 applied INSIDE every block.forward (L times), after E-step, before block norm
points.implication: Structurally divergent for n_layers>1: V2 interleaves the head mixer with the E-step at each of L layers (so it shapes the belief fed to the next block's prior), V3 applies a single mixer to the final converged belief only. Identical only at the default n_layers=1. When use_head_mixer is on with depth>1 the two models compute different functions. Both default OFF, so the pure path is unaffected.

points.topic: Outer/M-step training objective composition
points.v3: model.py:413-538: loss=ce, plus optional mass_phi (420), mstep_self_coupling_weight*sum alpha_i D (421-462), lambda_h*KL(s||r) (463-487), gamma_coupling*F_red^s (488-538); NO per-block aux scalar
points.v2: model.py:322-347: loss=ce_loss(+1/sqrt(K) scale), plus mass_phi penalty, plus per-block sum of block.e_step._aux_hyperparam_loss detached-F scalars routing grads to raw_c0/raw_b0/log_kappa
points.implication: Different M-step mechanism and different scalars the optimizer sees. V2's hyperparameter learning is a detached cached-F auxiliary loop (raw_c0/raw_b0/log_kappa); V3 threads learned params (log_alpha/log_lambda_beta/connection_W) through the unrolled E-step instead, and adds explicit free-energy-channel loss terms (self-coupling, hyper-prior, model-coupling) that V2's forward never assembles. V2 also has a normalize_ce_by_dim 1/sqrt(K) loss scale (model.py:316-318) with no V3 analog in this file. The CE core matches; the regularizer/hyperparam routing is a genuine architectural rewrite.

points.topic: Causal attention encoding: additive log-prior (V3) vs multiplicative tril mask (V2)
points.v3: model.py:315 log_prior=_attention_log_prior(N,...); attention_prior.py:59-64 causal prior returns 0 for j<=i, -inf for j>i (added inside softmax: beta=softmax_j(log_prior - E/tau))
points.v2: model.py:193-194 mask=torch.tril(ones(N,N)).expand(B,-1,-1) passed as (B,N,N) into stack->block->e_step (block.py:231, e_step mask= consumers)
points.implication: Same causal semantics by a different encoding: V3's additive 0/-inf log-prior inside the softmax is the log-domain equivalent of V2's multiplicative lower-triangular mask. V3 also caches the log-prior keyed on (name,N,device,dtype) (model.py:236-240) and supports a registry of priors (causal/etc.), generalizing V2's hardcoded tril. Behaviorally equivalent for the causal default; V3's prior registry is a modularity refactor.

points.topic: Batched vs per-sequence E-step execution
points.v3: model.py:355-360 vfe_stack runs with a leading B axis threaded through transport/gradients/retraction (vectorized over batch; pinned by test_perf_equivalence)
points.v2: stack.py:88-178 / block.py:199-259 forward carries (B,N,...) beliefs; E-step kernels operate batched via mask (B,N,N)
points.implication: Both process the batch in one set of kernels rather than a Python per-sequence loop; V3's docstring/test explicitly assert batched==per-sample. No numerical difference — a performance/structure refactor, with V3 documenting the equivalence as a golden constraint.

points.topic: Gauge frame phi: where it lives and is composed
points.v3: model.py:314 beliefs._replace(phi=_apply_pos_phi(beliefs.phi)); phi flows through BeliefState into the E-step transport; prior phi is never handed off (stack.py only mu_p/sigma_p)
points.v2: model.py:168-170 beliefs._replace(phi=pos_enc(beliefs.phi,N)); stack.py:167-170 new_prior_phi=initial_priors.phi (frozen, never consumed by E-step); phi cascades via beliefs.phi
points.implication: Identical phi treatment: positional element composed into phi once after encode, then phi flows through the belief (not the prior) so transport Omega=exp(phi_i)exp(-phi_j) is built from current belief phi at both endpoints. Both deliberately freeze/ignore prior phi in the cross-layer handoff. V3 adds an optional pos_phi='learned' BCH table and gauge-RoPE rotation threaded alongside (model.py:183-204, 242-258), absent from this V2 file's pos path but architecturally the same seam.

points.topic: Final / block normalization placement (Mahalanobis, gauge-equivariant)
points.v3: model.py:367-368 final_norm(mu_final,sigma_final) after head_mixer; block.py:65-66 block_norm(out.mu,out.sigma) after E-step; norm instances built once (model.py:89-92)
points.v2: model.py:200-211 final_norm(mu_final,beliefs.sigma) after stack; block.py:242-257 self.norm after E-step (+head_mixer); _resolve_vfe_norm
points.implication: Same placement and ordering on the post-E-step path: norm consumes (mu,sigma) and runs after the E-step (block-level) and after the stack (final-level), in both the head_mixer-then-norm order. Default mahalanobis/gauge-equivariant norm is the pure path in both. Difference is V2's full-cov ridge threads exp(phi) (_needs_exp_phi, model.py:129-133 / block.py:185-189) to keep the ridge GL(K)-covariant; V3's norm in this file does not pass exp_phi (diagonal default makes it moot).

points.topic: Pre-E-step normalization slot (norm_pre)
points.v3: n/a - absent (vfe_block has only the post-E-step block_norm at block.py:65-66; no pre-E-step norm slot)
points.v2: block.py:168-173, 221-229 self.norm_pre = _resolve_vfe_norm(norm_type_pre,'none'); applied to beliefs.mu BEFORE e_step when set (VFE_1 parity LayerNorm-before-update)
points.implication: V2 carries an opt-in pre-E-step normalization slot (default 'none', so dormant) that V3's block does not implement. Only matters if norm_type_pre is set; at the default both run E-step-first with no pre-norm, so this is an absent ablation knob, not a divergence on the pure path.

points.topic: Decode path: fused chunked-vocab decode + AMP fp32 island
points.v3: model.py:386-412: fused_chunked branch (decode_ce_diagonal_chunked, logits=None) never materializes (B,N,V); _amp_off_context fp32 island with .float() on decode/CE inputs; all-ignore microbatch guard (412)
points.v2: model.py:213-232 single decode (prior_bank.decode with decode_tau, or output_proj linear); CE in forward() model.py:298-308 with no chunking/AMP island
points.implication: V3 adds a memory-saving fused chunked decode+CE that skips the (B,N,V) logit tensor on the training path (logits returned as None) plus an explicit fp32 island for the decode/CE under optional AMP. V2 always materializes full logits and has no AMP path. Equal to the unchunked decode to atol-1e-3 by V3's own test; a numerics-preserving performance/precision refactor. V2 also has a coupled-filter roll_forward decode branch (model.py:222-224) outside V3's scope.

points.topic: Initial-prior snapshot for handoff (clone discipline)
points.v3: model.py:356 vfe_stack(beliefs, beliefs.mu, beliefs.sigma, ...) — passes the encode mu/sigma directly as the initial prior; handoff blends in stack.py
points.v2: model.py:186-190 initial_priors=BeliefState(mu, sigma.clone(), phi.clone()) — clones sigma/phi to defend against in-place mutation before stack
points.implication: Same logical setup (encode belief seeds the layer-0 prior), but V2 explicitly clones sigma/phi to guard against in-place writes, whereas V3 relies on its E-step being fully out-of-place and passes the tensors directly. No numerical difference given V3's out-of-place kernels; a defensive-copy refactor.

verdict.topic: Top-level forward pipeline (encode -> positional phi -> stack -> final norm -> decode -> CE)
verdict.evidence_v3: vfe3/model/model.py:413 "loss = ce" then 539 "return logits, loss, ce.detach()" — optimizer loss is the UNSCALED CE; no normalize-by-dim factor exists. config.py:205 "decode_mode: str = \"diagonal\"" (default takes the plain decode->F.cross_entropy branch at model.py:399-407, not the atol-1e-3 chunked path). mass_phi term model.py:420 "(out.phi ** 2).mean()". No "normalize_ce_by_dim" symbol exists anywhere in vfe3 code.
verdict.evidence_v2: config.py:327 "normalize_ce_by_dim: bool = True   # Divide CE by sqrt(K)". model.py:316-318 "if self.cfg.normalize_ce_by_dim: loss_scale = 1.0 / (self.cfg.embed_dim ** 0.5); ce_loss = ce_loss * loss_scale" then 322 "loss = ce_loss". mass_phi term model.py:335 "(beliefs.phi ** 2).sum() / (beliefs.phi.shape[0] * beliefs.phi.shape[1])".
verdict.finding: The logits-producing pipeline (encode -> positional phi -> stack -> final norm -> decode -> CE) is genuinely the same up to refactor on the default path: V3 decode_mode defaults to "diagonal", so it routes through prior_bank.decode -> F.cross_entropy exactly like V2, and the decode-tau apparent gap is a non-difference (V3's decode() defaults tau=None -> self._tau_eff -> decode_tau, V2 passes tau=decode_tau explicitly; both resolve to decode_tau). The reported CE is also equivalent: both detach the unscaled CE for logging (V2 ce_for_log at model.py:308 pre-scale; V3 ce.detach() at 539), so PPL = exp(ce) and BPC match. The golden-test-pinned object (logits) is a faithful reimplementation. The "same" claim nonetheless fails at the terminal step it explicitly names ("-> F.cross_entropy" producing the loss): the OPTIMIZER-facing loss differs by a constant factor on the DEFAULT path. V2 scales the gradient-bearing CE by 1/sqrt(embed_dim) because normalize_ce_by_dim defaults True; V3 has no such scaling and no such config knob at all, so its optimizer loss is the raw CE. This default-active factor changes the effective learning-rate/regularizer balance the M-step sees. Two further (default-off, hence non-refuting on their own but worth recording) divergences sit in the same loss block: V3 adds three V3-only loss channels absent from V2 (mstep_self_coupling_weight at model.py:421-462, lambda_h hyper-prior at 463-487, gamma_coupling model-channel at 488-538), and the mass_phi penalty uses .mean() over all axes in V3 vs .sum()/(B*N) in V2 (a factor-of-n_gen coefficient difference). V2's _aux_hyperparam_loss term has no V3 default-path counterpart (V3 threads log_alpha directly and lacks the cached aux scalar). Net: inference/logits pipeline = faithful refactor (golden tests hold); the optimizer training loss is divergent on the default path, so the macro architecture is NOT equivalent end-to-end as the high-significance "same" claim asserts.

verifiedHighPoints.topic: Cross-block MEAN handoff mu_q -> mu_p recurrence
verifiedHighPoints.v3: stack.py:47,54 rho=cfg.prior_handoff_rho; mu_p = (1.0-rho)*mu_p + rho*belief.mu each layer (always the blend form)
verifiedHighPoints.v2: stack.py:121-126 rho_mu=prior_handoff_rho; at rho==1.0 new_prior_mu=beliefs.mu (alias), else (1-rho)*priors.mu + rho*beliefs.mu
verifiedHighPoints.implication: Same recurrence: both blend the running prior mean against the just-converged belief mean. At the default rho=1.0 V2 takes the aliasing fast path (new_prior_mu=beliefs.mu) and V3 computes 0*mu_p+1*belief.mu; numerically identical. Defaults match (rho=1.0). This is the core belief-to-prior cascade that must agree with V2.

verdict.topic: Cross-block MEAN handoff mu_q -> mu_p recurrence
verdict.evidence_v3: vfe3/model/stack.py:47,54 — "rho = cfg.prior_handoff_rho" then in the per-layer loop "mu_p = (1.0 - rho) * mu_p + rho * belief.mu" (always the blend form; mu_p is the running prior, reassigned every iteration). Default rho=1.0 from config.py:215 "prior_handoff_rho: float = 1.0".
verdict.evidence_v2: transformer/vfe/stack.py:121-126 — "rho_mu = self.prior_handoff_rho; if rho_mu == 1.0: new_prior_mu = beliefs.mu  else: new_prior_mu = (1 - rho_mu) * priors.mu + rho_mu * beliefs.mu". priors is the running prior (reassigned at stack.py:172 "priors = BeliefState(mu=new_prior_mu, ...)" each loop). Default rho=1.0 from config.py:256 "prior_handoff_rho: float = 1.0".
verdict.finding: The MEAN recurrence is genuinely identical; the equivalence survives the adversarial attack. Both implementations blend the SAME operands: the running prior mean (V2 priors.mu, reassigned at stack.py:172 each loop; V3 mu_p, reassigned at stack.py:54 each loop) against the just-converged belief mean, with coefficient (1-rho) on the prior and rho on the belief. Defaults match exactly (rho=1.0 in both configs). At the default, V2 takes the aliasing fast path (new_prior_mu = beliefs.mu) while V3 evaluates 0.0*mu_p + 1.0*belief.mu; for finite tensors these are bit-identical (mul by 1.0 is exact; 0.0*finite + x = x). No clamp, eps, sign, coefficient, or power asymmetry exists on the mean path in either repo. Gradient flow matches: V3's 0.0*mu_p term contributes exactly zero gradient through the old prior (d/d(mu_p)[0.0*mu_p]=0), and at rho<1 the (1-rho) coefficient on the running prior matches V2. Two non-behavioral edge details exist and do not break equivalence under a finite forward pass: (1) V2 aliases the belief tensor whereas V3 produces a fresh tensor (no output/gradient consequence since the E-step is fully out-of-place in both); (2) a degenerate inf/NaN in mu_p would make V3's 0.0*mu_p = NaN propagate where V2's alias would not — but that does not arise in normal operation. SCOPE NOTE (does not change this verdict): the sibling SIGMA handoff is NOT structurally parallel — V2 blends sigma against the frozen embedding initial_priors.sigma (stack.py:142,131) while V3 blends against the running sigma_p (stack.py:55); they agree only at the rho_sigma=0 default and diverge for rho_sigma in (0,1). That is a separate claim and leaves the MEAN relationship = same.

verifiedHighPoints.topic: Head mixer application frequency: per-block (V2) vs once-after-stack (V3)
verifiedHighPoints.v3: model.py:96 head_mixer built once; model.py:364-365 applied ONCE after vfe_stack, before final_norm
verifiedHighPoints.v2: block.py:153-156 mixer built per VFEBlock; block.py:238-240 applied INSIDE every block.forward (L times), after E-step, before block norm
verifiedHighPoints.implication: Structurally divergent for n_layers>1: V2 interleaves the head mixer with the E-step at each of L layers (so it shapes the belief fed to the next block's prior), V3 applies a single mixer to the final converged belief only. Identical only at the default n_layers=1. When use_head_mixer is on with depth>1 the two models compute different functions. Both default OFF, so the pure path is unaffected.

verdict.topic: Head mixer application frequency: per-block (V2) vs once-after-stack (V3)
verdict.evidence_v2: block.py:238-240 head mixer applied inside VFEBlock.forward after e_step (block.py:231): mixer is not None branch sets mu_mixed/sigma_mixed and replaces beliefs; built per-block at block.py:153-156. stack.py:84-86 builds L blocks; stack.py:114-115 loops over self.blocks calling block(beliefs, priors, mask), so the mixer fires L times; post-mixer belief.mu becomes the next layer prior at stack.py:120-124.
verdict.evidence_v3: model.py:364-365 head mixer applied ONCE to stack output (mu_final, sigma_final) before final_norm; stack.py:49-53 loops for _ in range(cfg.n_layers) calling only vfe_block with no mixer inside; model.py:96 head_mixer built once when cfg.use_head_mixer.
verdict.finding: Refutation fails; divergence holds. Verified actual code in both repos (V3 model.py is at vfe3/model/model.py, not root; cited lines map correctly). V3 applies the head mixer exactly once in VFE3Model.forward (model.py:364-365) to the converged belief from vfe_stack, before final_norm; the per-layer loop (stack.py:49-53) calls only vfe_block with no mixer (confirmed by repo-wide grep excluding stack.py). V2 builds a mixer per VFEBlock (block.py:153-156) and applies it inside every block.forward after the E-step (block.py:238-240); VFEStack.forward iterates L blocks (stack.py:114-115), so the mixer fires L times, and the post-mixer belief.mu feeds the next layer prior at stack.py:120-124. For n_layers>1 the two compute different functions; they coincide only at default n_layers=1. Both toggles default OFF, so the pure no-NN path is identical and unaffected. The divergence is conditional (depth-gated and toggle-gated) but the divergent label and the implication scoping to use_head_mixer plus n_layers>1 are accurate.
