verdict.evidence_v2: VFE_2.0/math_utils/generators/builders.py:76 "def generate_so3_generators" (tesseral spin-ℓ, K=2ℓ+1, Casimir -ΣG_a²=ℓ(ℓ+1)I at :95/:277-283); :804 "def generate_wedge2_generators" (Λ²V); :947 "def generate_sym2_traceless_generators" (Sym²₀V); :307 "def generate_multi_irrep_generators(irrep_spec: list" (block-diag SO(3) ⊕_ℓ V_ℓ^{n_ℓ}); :680 "def generate_multi_irrep_soN_generators(irrep_spec: list, N: int" supporting scalar/fund/wedge2/sym2. All five publicly exported in __init__.py:11-14,37-40.
verdict.finding: The claim holds; the adversarial attack to refute it fails. V3's only SO(N) builder, generate_son (generators.py:258), constructs strictly the fundamental skew generators L_ij=E_ij-E_ji and registers them as a single block (irrep_dims=[K]); a package-wide search of vfe3/ for so3/wedge2/sym2/tesseral/irrep_spec/spin yields no builder anywhere, and generators.py:257 explicitly TODOs the missing SO(N)-irreps-as-heads. V2 implements all four cited symbols at their cited lines — Casimir-validated spin-ℓ (so3), Λ²V (wedge2), Sym²₀V (sym2), and two multi-irrep block-diagonal assemblers (SO(3) at :307, SO(N) at :680 over irrep_spec=[(label,mult,dim)] with scalar/fund/wedge2/sym2) — all re-exported as public API. The relationship is a genuine v2_only capability gap. The ONLY inaccuracy in the original point is a cosmetic path-prefix typo: the V2 file is at VFE_2.0/math_utils/generators/builders.py, NOT VFE_2.0/transformer/vfe/math_utils/generators/builders.py — but every cited line number (76, 804, 947, 307, 680) matches the correct file exactly, so no symbol was misread. No subtle equivalence hides under the v2_only label: V3's so_k/son cannot represent any non-fundamental SO(N) irrep nor a block-diagonal multi-irrep SO group, exactly as claimed. The user's compared default (irrep_spec=[('fund',2,10)], gauge_group='GLK') indeed uses none of this V2 SO(N) machinery, as the point already notes.

verifiedHighPoints.topic: Lie-coordinate projection / bracket: unified gram-pinv vs three parallel families
verifiedHighPoints.v3: vfe3/geometry/lie_ops.py:43 extract_phi (Gram⁺ least-squares), :25 gram_pinv (float64), :69 lie_bracket_coords — ONE generic path for all groups
verifiedHighPoints.v2: math_utils/generators/lie_ops.py:88 extract_soN_coords_torch, :391 extract_glK_coords_torch, :268 lie_bracket_general_torch — three parallel per-group families
verifiedHighPoints.implication: Behavioral divergence in the projection route. V2's extract_glK has a fast-path A.reshape when n_gen==K*K (builders comment MU-1: 145% round-trip error / silent BCH corruption for a non-E_ij basis at exactly K²). V3's extract_phi ALWAYS solves Gram⁺·g, so it is correct for arbitrary (closed/overcomplete) bases and has no fast-path trap. For the standard E_ij basis both give the same coords; they diverge only off the E_ij basis (closure/sp), where V3 is the correct one.

verdict.topic: Lie-coordinate projection / bracket: unified Gram-pinv (V3) vs three parallel per-group families (V2)
verdict.evidence_v3: C:/Users/chris and christine/Desktop/V3_Transformer/vfe3/geometry/lie_ops.py:57-58 extract_phi: "g = torch.einsum(\"aij,...ij->...a\", generators, matrix)" then "return torch.einsum(\"...a,ab->...b\", g, gp)" with gp = gram_pinv (full Gram-pinv least-squares); :66 lie_bracket_matrix "return A @ B - B @ A"
verdict.evidence_v2: Actual path is C:/Users/chris and christine/Desktop/VFE_2.0/math_utils/generators/lie_ops.py (NOT under transformer/vfe/ as cited; same fn names/lines 88/268/391). :412-414 extract_glK_coords_torch fast path "if n_gen == K * K:" -> "return A.reshape(batch_shape + (K * K,))"; :423-427 general path "coords = A_flat2 @ G_flat.T" then "coords = coords / norms_sq...clamp(min=1e-12)" (DIAGONAL normalization, not full Gram); :285 bracket "bracket = A1 @ A2 - A2 @ A1"
verdict.finding: The refactor claim survives the adversarial attack. Empirically verified four ways. (1) On the standard E_ij basis (n_gen=K*K, Gram=I) V3 extract_phi and V2 extract_glK_coords_torch give byte-identical coords (max abs diff 0.0, both round-trip err 0.0) -- the on-basis equivalence the claim asserts is TRUE because for an orthogonal basis the full Gram-pinv solve reduces exactly to V2's per-generator diagonal normalization <G_a,A>/||G_a||^2. (2) The bracket sign AB-BA is identical in both (V2:285, V3:66) and the degree-5 Dynkin coefficients match (1/2, 1/12, 1/24, 1/720, 1/360, 1/120) after applying [X,Y]=-[Y,X]. (3) The one structural difference the claim OMITS -- V2's soN_bracket_torch builds its own N×N defining-rep generators internally and ignores the passed K×K generators, whereas V3 brackets in the passed transport rep -- resolves BENIGNLY: feeding the same so(3) defining generators to V3's generic lie_bracket_coords reproduces V2's soN_bracket_torch exactly (max diff 0.0). So it is a faithful consolidation, not a divergence, when the so(N) defining rep is the transport rep. (4) The claimed off-basis divergence is real and correctly attributed to V3 being the correct path: on a non-orthogonal basis V3 recovers true coords (round-trip err ~1e-6, matches torch.linalg.lstsq) while V2's general path gives wrong coords (round-trip err 2.5); on a generic non-orthogonal basis at exactly n_gen==K*K, V2's fast-path A.reshape silently corrupts (114% relative round-trip error here; the MU-1 comment cites 145% for the actual auto_close basis) while V3 extract_phi is exact. No unacknowledged on-basis difference, no sign/coefficient/power error, no different eps/clamp affecting the standard-basis result was found. Caveats not bearing on the projection/bracket scope: the cited V2 path is wrong (file is at math_utils/generators/ not transformer/vfe/math_utils/, though fn names and line numbers 88/268/391 match), and the retraction defaults differ (V3 mode="euclidean"=addition vs V2 bch_order=4; V3 max_norm=math.pi vs V2 3.14159) -- those are retraction functions outside the cited extract/bracket scope.

dimensions.summary: The two codebases implement the SAME core differential geometry — the flat phi-cocycle transport Omega_ij = exp(phi_i)exp(-phi_j), the affine-invariant SPD exponential-map retraction, the BCH gauge-frame retraction, and group-default trust regions/max-norms all match V2 by construction (V3 is the golden-pinned reimplementation). V3 is a leaner, registry-driven refactor that factors the transport into per-vertex exps (a fast diagonal-sandwich path) and adds genuinely NEW variants V2 lacks: a log-Euclidean SPD retraction, a position-dependent pullback phi preconditioner, and a true gauge-sandwich block-diagonal RoPE Omega^RoPE = R_i Omega_ij R_j^T. The non-flat connection survives in both but with materially DIVERGENT parameterizations: V2's regularization-heavy bilinear (antisymmetrized + block-masked + 1/d_h + learned tanh strength gate + per-edge Frobenius clamp) versus V3's bare cocycle-relaxation delta = relaxation * mu^T W mu. A real numerical divergence: V2 clamps full-cov eigenvalues to sigma_max^2 while V3 clamps to sigma_max.

points.topic: Flat (Regime-I) transport operator Omega_ij = exp(phi_i)exp(-phi_j)
points.v3: vfe3/geometry/transport.py:301-340 compute_transport_operators builds exp_phi/exp_neg_phi then omega = einsum('bikl,bjlm->bijkm') in GL+(K); 'trivial' returns I
points.v2: transformer/core/transport_ops.py:11-13 header 'Omega_ij = exp(phi_i*G)*exp(-phi_j*G)'; per-block exps via fused_block_matrix_exp_pairs (referenced from non_flat.py:382-392)
points.implication: The canonical flat gauge transport — the geometry the whole attention/free-energy coupling rides on — is mathematically identical. This is the equivalence the golden tests pin; both compute exp(phi_i)exp(-phi_j) as the cocycle Omega, so Omega_ii=I and the sandwich action on beliefs matches.

points.topic: Factored transport + per-head diagonal sandwich fast path
points.v3: vfe3/geometry/transport.py:343-375 build_factored_transport keeps only per-token exps; transport_mean:404-406 fuses m_j=exp(-phi_j)mu_j then exp(phi_i)m_j; _factored_diagonal_covariance:458-480 sandwiches per irrep block (H*d^2 not K^2)
points.v2: n/a - absent (V2 materializes per-block dense Omega in compute_pairwise_omega_with_delta non_flat.py:442-446 and contracts mu/sigma against it; no fused vertex-exp container)
points.implication: A pure performance refactor: V3's mean fusion is an exact reassociation (round-off level) and the block-diagonal sandwich is exact because off-block Omega entries are 0.0, so it is numerically the same transport but avoids the dense (B,N,N,K,K) tensor. Behavior-preserving, not a geometry change.

points.topic: Non-flat connection: edge factor exp(delta_ij.G) sandwiched between vertex exps
points.v3: vfe3/geometry/transport.py:147,195 Omega_ij = exp(phi_i) exp(delta_ij.G) exp(-phi_j) via einsum('bikl,bijlm,bjmn->bijkn'); delta.G built at transport.py:186
points.v2: transformer/vfe/non_flat.py:344,442-446 Omega^(h)_ij = exp(phi_i.G^h) exp(delta_ij.G^h) exp(-phi_j.G^h) per block (exp_phi.unsqueeze(2) @ exp_delta @ exp_neg_phi.unsqueeze(1))
points.implication: The edge-relaxed cocycle STRUCTURE (vertex-edge-vertex triple product, delta living in the group's own generator coordinates so exp(delta.G) stays in the group) is the same Regime-II connection in both. The triangle holonomy this introduces is the curvature both holonomy diagnostics read.

points.topic: Non-flat delta PARAMETERIZATION (the bilinear and its regularization)
points.v3: vfe3/geometry/transport.py:184 delta = cocycle_relaxation * einsum('bik,akl,bjl->bija', mu, connection_W, mu) — RAW connection_W, single homotopy scalar, no antisymmetrization/mask/clamp/1-over-d_h
points.v2: transformer/vfe/non_flat.py:294-312 delta = strength * (1/d_h) * einsum(mu, antisym(W*block_mask), mu) with strength=max_strength*tanh(rho) (line 252) and a per-edge Cauchy-Schwarz Frobenius clamp (308-312)
points.implication: Genuinely different connections off zero-init. V2 forces W antisymmetric and block-masked (delta is antisymmetric in i,j, so Omega_ij^-1 ties to Omega_ji and det stays controlled), gates non-flatness behind a learned tanh strength so the optimizer must move BOTH rho and W, and per-edge-clamps ||delta.G||_F. V3 uses bare W * a fixed cocycle_relaxation homotopy scalar with NONE of these. Both collapse to the flat cocycle exactly at W=0/relaxation=0 (the golden oracle), but a trained nonzero connection produces a different delta tensor, different holonomy, and V3 leans entirely on stable_matrix_exp_pair's global Frobenius clamp (max_norm=15) rather than a per-edge bound.

points.topic: delta antisymmetry / det control
points.v3: vfe3/geometry/transport.py:184 uses connection_W as-is — no 0.5*(W - W^T); delta_ij is not forced antisymmetric in (i,j)
points.v2: transformer/vfe/non_flat.py:266-267 W() returns 0.5*(W_masked - W_masked.transpose(-1,-2)) every forward, giving delta[i,j,a] = -delta[j,i,a] (non_flat.py:289)
points.implication: V2's antisymmetrization makes the edge connection a proper skew form so the reverse edge is the algebraic inverse; V3 drops it, so V3's delta has no enforced (i,j) antisymmetry and the edge factor is a more general (less constrained) group element. Affects which holonomies are representable and the symmetry of the learned connection.

points.topic: Full SPD retraction: affine-invariant exponential map
points.v3: vfe3/geometry/retraction.py:115-174 retract_spd_full: Sigma^{1/2} exp(Sigma^{-1/2}(tau dSigma)Sigma^{-1/2}) Sigma^{1/2} with Frobenius trust region on whitened tangent, two eigh, eps floor
points.v2: transformer/core/vfe_utils.py:636-725 retract_spd_torch: identical affine-invariant map, same whitening R=Sigma^{-1/2} dSigma Sigma^{-1/2}, same Frobenius trust region, same two eigh structure
points.implication: The manuscript-canonical SPD geometry is the same operator in both (V3's spd_affine default == V2's retract_spd_torch). This is a core belief-update primitive the golden tests must pin; structurally byte-for-byte the same algorithm under a renamed function.

points.topic: Full-cov eigenvalue CEILING: sigma_max vs sigma_max^2
points.v3: vfe3/geometry/retraction.py:168 eig_new.clamp(min=eps, max=sigma_max) with comment 'eigenvalues ARE variances: ONE ceiling' (same un-squared ceiling as the diagonal arm)
points.v2: transformer/core/vfe_utils.py:710,716 _sigma_max_sq = sigma_max*sigma_max; eig_new.clamp(min=eps, max=_sigma_max_sq) — full-cov ceiling is sigma_max^2, while the diagonal path (vfe_utils.py:784) clamps to sigma_max
points.implication: A REAL numerical divergence in the full-covariance retraction. V2 treats sigma_max as a std-dev (eigenvalues = variances bounded by sigma_max^2) and is internally inconsistent (diag clamps the variance to sigma_max, full clamps to sigma_max^2). V3 unifies both arms to clamp the variance/eigenvalue to sigma_max. At default sigma_max=5.0, V2's full-cov ceiling is 25 vs V3's 5 — different SPD trust regions; full-cov runs are NOT byte-identical between V2 and V3.

points.topic: Diagonal SPD retraction sigma_new = sigma*exp(tau*clamp(dsigma/sigma))
points.v3: vfe3/geometry/retraction.py:89-112 retract_spd_diagonal: whiten dsigma/sigma, trust-region clamp, exp_arg clamp [-50,50], ceiling sigma_max
points.v2: transformer/core/vfe_utils.py:728-784 retract_spd_diagonal_torch: identical whitening, trust-region clamp default 5.0, exp_arg clamp [-50,50], ceiling sigma_max
points.implication: The diagonal affine retraction (the default belief-cone update) is the same elementwise reduction of the affine map in both, same clamps and same un-squared sigma_max ceiling. Equivalent.

points.topic: Log-Euclidean SPD retraction Sigma_new = expm(logm(Sigma) + step*sym(dlog))
points.v3: vfe3/geometry/retraction.py:210-319 retract_logeuclidean_full + retract_log_euclidean (registered 'log_euclidean'); SPD-preserving for any tangent magnitude (no trust region needed for positivity)
points.v2: n/a - absent (only the affine-invariant retract_spd_torch exists; grep for log_euclidean over transformer/ hits only configs/ablation strings, no retraction kernel)
points.implication: A genuinely new SPD geometry variant in V3 only, selectable via the retraction registry. For full covariance logm != elementwise log so it is a distinct geodesic; the V3 docstring itself notes that on a diagonal family with the pre-whitened (affine Fisher) tangent it does NOT coincide with spd_affine, so it is a non-canonical option there. Does not change the default (pure) path, which remains spd_affine == V2.

points.topic: eigh degenerate-spectrum backward regularization (_EighDamped)
points.v3: vfe3/geometry/retraction.py:39-87 _EighDamped: custom autograd.Function replacing 1/(lambda_i-lambda_j) with Lorentzian Delta/(Delta^2+gap_eps) so the Sigma=I init gives finite (not NaN) gradients; forward bit-identical to eigh
points.v2: transformer/core/vfe_utils.py:681 _safe_eigh(Sigma, jitter=eps) — forward-side jitter on the matrix before eigh, no custom gap-regularized backward
points.implication: Different stabilization philosophy for the eigendecomposition adjoint. V3 fixes the backward (the gap term) while keeping the forward exact; V2 jitters the forward input. Matters for the unrolled-E-step gradient at degenerate spectra (the isotropic prior init), where V3 claims finite gradients without perturbing forward values; the two will give slightly different gradients through the SPD retraction even when forward Sigma matches.

points.topic: Gauge-frame (phi) retraction: chart (BCH vs euclidean) and group defaults
points.v3: vfe3/geometry/retraction.py:359-403 retract_phi dispatches retract_son/retract_glk; defaults trust_region GL=0.1/SO=0.3, max_norm GL=5.0/SO=pi (lines 384-387); mode 'euclidean'|'bch' via compose_phi (lie_ops.py:161-250)
points.v2: transformer/core/vfe_utils.py:811-911 retract_phi_torch auto-selects bch_order=4, GL trust_region=0.1/max_norm=5.0, SO trust_region=0.3/max_norm=pi (comments 823-824, code 867-868)
points.implication: The Lie-algebra phi update uses the same chart options (euclidean step or BCH correction) and the SAME group-compactness-driven trust_region/max_norm defaults in both. det control (project_slk / trace clamp for GL, automatic for skew SO) matches. Equivalent geometry, refactored into a registry-based composition dispatcher.

points.topic: Positional-phi BCH injection into the gauge frame
points.v3: vfe3/model/positional_phi.py:106-135 apply_positional_phi composes per-position coords into phi via compose_phi (mode='bch' default, order=4) BEFORE transport; registry 'none'/'learned'/'frozen'; optional project_slk for det=1
points.v2: transformer/vfe/positional.py:118-146 VFEPositionalEncoding.forward composes token phi with pos_phi_free via lie_compose_bch_general_torch (bch_order); bch_order<=1 is additive phi+pos; phi_project_slk stores sl(K) free coords for det=1
points.implication: Same idea and same math — position is a Lie-algebra element composed into the frame by BCH so it enters through Omega_ij and Omega_ii=I is untouched, with traceless (sl(K)) projection preserving det(Omega_h)=1. V3 refactors it into a config registry with a parameter-free 'frozen' Lie-ALiBi variant V2 lacks; V2 carries an abelian first-order (additive) fallback that V3's bch path replaces. Functionally the same positional gauge composition.

points.topic: Gauge-RoPE: true transport sandwich vs mu-only pre-KL rotation
points.v3: vfe3/geometry/rope.py:47-78 build_rope_rotation: BLOCK-DIAGONAL R(theta) within each irrep block (pairs theta=n*base^{-2k/d}, default base=100), applied as Omega^RoPE_ij = R_i Omega_ij R_j^T (transport.py:74-79,398-403); covariance rotated only when on_cov/rope_full_gauge (transport.py:438-443)
points.v2: transformer/core/transport_ops.py:115-178 _apply_rope rotates mu by its own absolute position (pairs across full K, theta=base^{-2k/K}, default base=10000) BEFORE the KL (non_flat.py:517-519), leaving Sigma raw; _apply_rope_to_covariance:181-234 is the separate experimental rope_full_gauge sandwich
points.implication: Materially different RoPE realizations. V3 folds the rotation into the transport OPERATOR as a genuine gauge sandwich R_i Omega_ij R_j^T (block-diagonal within irreps, preserving the block-exp fast path, base=100), so it is a coherent gauge action; V2 rotates only the scoring means pre-KL across the full K with base=10000 (the standard Q/K pattern, which V2's own docstring flags as neither the full gauge interp nor a textbook rotated-Gaussian KL). Both expose a full-gauge toggle (V3 on_cov/rope_full_gauge, V2 rope_full_gauge) that adds the Sigma sandwich. Different frequency basis (per-block d vs full K), different default base, and different operator placement mean RoPE outputs are NOT equivalent between the two.

points.topic: Pre-exp Frobenius safeguard on the algebra before matrix_exp
points.v3: vfe3/geometry/transport.py:199-260 stable_matrix_exp_pair: global Frobenius clamp to max_norm=15 + float64 upcast + block-diagonal exp; rescales WHOLE matrix, returns exp(max_norm*M/||M||) when exceeded
points.v2: transformer/vfe/_numerics.py:175-221 pre_exp_frobenius_clamp: per-slice straight-through Frobenius rescale to max_fro (opt-in via phi_spec_max, non_flat.py:403-404), bounds ||exp(X)||_2 for the sandwich condition number
points.implication: Both bound the Lie-algebra Frobenius norm before matrix_exp for stability and both are safeguards (not the exact operator) that bind only on extreme inputs. V3 bakes a default global clamp (max_norm=15) plus float64 upcast and a block-diagonal exp into one helper; V2 keeps it an opt-in per-slice clamp (phi_spec_max default None). Same numerical purpose, different default-on vs opt-in and global vs per-slice scope.

points.topic: phi-gradient preconditioner (killing / killing_per_block / pullback / clip / none)
points.v3: vfe3/geometry/phi_preconditioner.py:51-330 registry: none (identity), clip (norm-clip), killing (Cartan-involution metric 2K*gram - 2 tr tr, cached inverse), killing_per_block (block-diagonal Killing), pullback (position-dependent G_ab(phi)=<d exp_phi(T_a),d exp_phi(T_b)>_F via adaptive Psi(ad_phi) series)
points.v2: n/a - absent (V2 phi update applies trust_region/max_norm clamps in retract_phi_torch but has no Killing/pullback natural-gradient metric on the gauge-frame gradient within the named files; grep shows no killing/pullback preconditioner kernel)
points.implication: V3 adds a whole modular phi-gradient metric layer (Cartan Killing natural gradient and a position-dependent pullback metric) absent in V2's transport/positional/numerics files. The 'none' default keeps the canonical no-metric Lie-algebra update (pure path == V2's plain coordinate gradient), so the default behavior matches; the killing/pullback modes are new opt-in geometry. The docstring correctly flags Killing as Ad(K)-invariant only (gauge-equivariant under SO(N) but not non-compact GL(K) directions).

verdict.topic: Flat (Regime-I) transport operator Omega_ij = exp(phi_i)exp(-phi_j)
verdict.evidence_v3: vfe3/geometry/transport.py:339 `omega = torch.einsum("bikl,bjlm->bijkm", exp_phi, exp_neg_phi)`; phi_matrix at :332 `torch.einsum("bna,aij->bnij", phi, generators)`; clamp scope at :230 `mat_norm = matrix.norm(dim=(-2, -1), keepdim=True)` (FULL K x K before blockwise exp at :243-251)
verdict.evidence_v2: transformer/core/transport_ops.py:426 `Omega = torch.einsum('bikl,bjlm->bijkm', exp_phi, exp_neg_phi)`; phi_matrix at :375 `torch.einsum('bna,aij->bnij', phi, generators)`. BUT live attention path uses transformer/vfe/attention.py:117 `fused_block_matrix_exp_pairs` -> reshape `(n_blocks*B, N, d, d)` (gauge_utils.py:318) -> clamp PER-BLOCK at gauge_utils.py:106 `matrix.norm(dim=(-2, -1))`
verdict.finding: The mathematical operator is identical: both build exp_phi/exp_neg_phi from einsum('bna,aij->bnij', phi, generators) via the same stable_matrix_exp_pair (max_norm=15.0, dim_threshold=20, skew->transpose shortcut), then contract the cocycle with the IDENTICAL einsum 'bikl,bjlm->bijkm', giving Omega_ii=I and the same sign/index convention. Sandwich on beliefs matches. So the claimed equivalence is literally true and the golden tests legitimately pin it. One subtle difference hides under the claim that does NOT alter the operator but does alter numerics at the clamp boundary: the Frobenius-norm SAFETY clamp scope differs for block-diagonal (block_glk [d_head]*H) groups. V3's default flat path (compute_transport_operators / build_factored_transport) builds the full (B,N,K,K) phi_matrix and clamps ||M||_F over the WHOLE K x K matrix (transport.py:230) before taking the blockwise exp, so all heads share one global rescale factor. V2's live attention path (compute_gauge_transport -> fused_block_matrix_exp_pairs) slices phi into per-block (d,d) Lie matrices and clamps each head's norm INDEPENDENTLY (gauge_utils.py:106 over the reshaped per-block batch). When ||phi|| exceeds max_norm=15, V3 rescales every block by sqrt(sum_h ||phi_h.G_h||_F^2) while V2 rescales block h by ||phi_h.G_h||_F, producing different per-block exp(phi) and hence different Omega. Below the clamp (the documented exact regime where golden tests run) the two are bit-equivalent up to reassociation round-off, so the clamp-scope divergence is an extreme-input stability edge case, not a change in the transport math. Secondary note: the claim's V2 citation (transport_ops.py compute_transport_operators) is itself a full-matrix-clamp function matching V3 exactly; it is V2's OTHER function (fused_block_matrix_exp_pairs, used by the actual attention forward) that diverges in clamp scope -- the evidence conflates the two V2 code paths. Relationship 'same' survives because the operator and the pinned golden-test regime are identical; the only difference is a stability clamp on out-of-range inputs.

The triangle holonomy this introduces is the curvature both holonomy diagnostics read.

verdict.topic: Non-flat connection: edge factor exp(delta_ij.G) sandwiched between vertex exps
verdict.evidence_v3: vfe3/geometry/transport.py:184 `delta = cocycle_relaxation * torch.einsum("bik,akl,bjl->bija", mu, connection_W, mu)` (raw connection_W, no antisymmetrize/mask/1-over-d_h/gate); :195 `omega = torch.einsum("bikl,bijlm,bjmn->bijkn", exp_phi, exp_delta, exp_neg_phi)`; model.py:111 `self.connection_W = nn.Parameter(torch.zeros(n_gen, cfg.embed_dim, cfg.embed_dim))`
verdict.evidence_v2: non_flat.py:267 `return 0.5 * (W_masked - W_masked.transpose(-1, -2))`; :294-299 `B_anti = self.W(); delta_raw = einsum('bik,akl,bjl->bija', mu, B_anti, mu); delta = delta_raw * self.inv_d_h_per_gen...; delta = self.strength * delta`; :308-312 per-edge Frobenius clamp `scale = self.per_edge_delta_max / denom`; :442-446 `exp_phi_h.unsqueeze(2) @ exp_delta @ exp_neg_phi_h.unsqueeze(1)`
verdict.finding: The narrow structural claim is literally true: both wrap exp(delta_ij.G) between vertex factors exp(phi_i) and exp(-phi_j), and both seed delta via the SAME einsum string 'bik,akl,bjl->bija' with delta living in generator coordinates (V3 transport.py:195 einsum vs V2 non_flat.py:442-446 matmul are equivalent contractions; V3 :184 vs V2 :295 share the einsum). But "same"/"same Regime-II connection... same curvature" is false: the OPERATOR that produces delta differs materially. (1) Antisymmetrization: V2 feeds B_anti = 0.5*(W_masked - W^T) (non_flat.py:267), making delta antisymmetric in (i,j) (delta[i,j,a]=-delta[j,i,a], doc :288-289); V3 feeds RAW connection_W (transport.py:184) with no antisymmetrization, so V3's delta is NOT antisymmetric. (2) Block mask: V2 multiplies W by W_block_mask (non_flat.py:266); V3 has none. (3) Per-generator 1/d_h scaling: V2 multiplies by inv_d_h_per_gen (:298); V3 has none. (4) Strength gate: V2 multiplies by s_max*tanh(raw_strength) (:252,299), a learned two-stage gate; V3 has only the fixed scalar cocycle_relaxation (default 1.0). (5) Per-edge clamp: V2 clamps ||delta_ij.G||_F <= per_edge_delta_max per edge (:308-312); V3 has no per-edge delta clamp, relying only on the generic stable_matrix_exp_pair Frobenius clamp at max_norm=15 on the whole delta_mat (transport.py:190-192) — a different bound at a different stage. Consequence: for identical mu and numerically equal raw W, V3 and V2 produce DIFFERENT exp_delta, hence different Omega and different triangle holonomy; the holonomy diagnostics do NOT read the same curvature. Both reduce to the flat cocycle exactly at W=0 / cocycle_relaxation=0 (V3 transport.py:153-155, V2 init W=0 + tanh(0)=0), so they agree only at the trivial fixed point. The edge-relaxed cocycle SKELETON is shared; the connection is reimplemented as a reduced raw-bilinear operator. This is refactored, not same.

verifiedHighPoints.topic: Non-flat delta PARAMETERIZATION (the bilinear and its regularization)
verifiedHighPoints.v3: vfe3/geometry/transport.py:184 delta = cocycle_relaxation * einsum('bik,akl,bjl->bija', mu, connection_W, mu) — RAW connection_W, single homotopy scalar, no antisymmetrization/mask/clamp/1-over-d_h
verifiedHighPoints.v2: transformer/vfe/non_flat.py:294-312 delta = strength * (1/d_h) * einsum(mu, antisym(W*block_mask), mu) with strength=max_strength*tanh(rho) (line 252) and a per-edge Cauchy-Schwarz Frobenius clamp (308-312)
verifiedHighPoints.implication: Genuinely different connections off zero-init. V2 forces W antisymmetric and block-masked (delta is antisymmetric in i,j, so Omega_ij^-1 ties to Omega_ji and det stays controlled), gates non-flatness behind a learned tanh strength so the optimizer must move BOTH rho and W, and per-edge-clamps ||delta.G||_F. V3 uses bare W * a fixed cocycle_relaxation homotopy scalar with NONE of these. Both collapse to the flat cocycle exactly at W=0/relaxation=0 (the golden oracle), but a trained nonzero connection produces a different delta tensor, different holonomy, and V3 leans entirely on stable_matrix_exp_pair's global Frobenius clamp (max_norm=15) rather than a per-edge bound.

verdict.topic: Non-flat delta PARAMETERIZATION (the bilinear and its regularization)
verdict.evidence_v3: vfe3/geometry/transport.py:184 `delta = cocycle_relaxation * torch.einsum("bik,akl,bjl->bija", mu, connection_W, mu)` — RAW connection_W (defined model.py:111 `nn.Parameter(torch.zeros(n_gen, embed_dim, embed_dim))`); only a single config float cocycle_relaxation and the global Frobenius clamp in stable_matrix_exp_pair (transport.py:203 `max_norm: float = 15.0`). Grep over transport.py confirms NO antisym/block_mask/1-over-d_h/per-edge clamp in the connection path.
verdict.evidence_v2: non_flat.py:266-267 `W_masked = self.W_raw * self.W_block_mask` then `return 0.5 * (W_masked - W_masked.transpose(-1, -2))` (block mask + antisymmetrization); :252 `return self.max_strength * torch.tanh(self.raw_strength)` (learned tanh gate); :298-299 `delta = delta_raw * self.inv_d_h_per_gen...; delta = self.strength * delta` (1/d_h + strength); :310-312 `denom = fro_bound_sq.clamp(min=self.per_edge_delta_max ** 2).sqrt(); scale = self.per_edge_delta_max / denom; delta = delta * scale...` (per-edge Cauchy-Schwarz Frobenius clamp).
verdict.finding: Relationship survives the adversarial attack: genuinely divergent, high significance. V2 wraps the bilinear in five regularizers V3 entirely omits — antisymmetrization + block-mask on W (266-267), learned tanh strength gate on a SEPARATE raw_strength parameter (228/234/252), per-generator 1/d_h scaling (298), and a per-edge Frobenius clamp (308-312). V3 consumes connection_W RAW with only a single fixed homotopy scalar (cocycle_relaxation, default 1.0) and leans on the global max_norm=15 Frobenius clamp inside stable_matrix_exp_pair rather than a per-edge bound. No 'missing' V3 symbol exists under another name (grep confirms), and the comparing agent misread neither side. Both collapse to the flat cocycle exactly at zero (V3: connection_W=None or cocycle_relaxation==0 short-circuit at transport.py:179; V2: tanh(0)=0 or W_raw=0), but off zero-init the delta tensors, holonomy, and det control differ. One harmless citation slip in the original: it pins antisymmetrization to '294-312' but it actually lives in W() at 254-267 (294-312 is the bilinear+scaling+clamp); strength is correctly at :252. Substance unaffected — verdict stands.

verifiedHighPoints.topic: Full SPD retraction: affine-invariant exponential map
verifiedHighPoints.v3: vfe3/geometry/retraction.py:115-174 retract_spd_full: Sigma^{1/2} exp(Sigma^{-1/2}(tau dSigma)Sigma^{-1/2}) Sigma^{1/2} with Frobenius trust region on whitened tangent, two eigh, eps floor
verifiedHighPoints.v2: transformer/core/vfe_utils.py:636-725 retract_spd_torch: identical affine-invariant map, same whitening R=Sigma^{-1/2} dSigma Sigma^{-1/2}, same Frobenius trust region, same two eigh structure
verifiedHighPoints.implication: The manuscript-canonical SPD geometry is the same operator in both (V3's spd_affine default == V2's retract_spd_torch). This is a core belief-update primitive the golden tests must pin; structurally byte-for-byte the same algorithm under a renamed function.

verdict.topic: Full SPD retraction: affine-invariant exponential map
verdict.evidence_v3: vfe3/geometry/retraction.py:168 `eig_new = eig_new.clamp(min=eps, max=sigma_max)      # eigenvalues ARE variances: ONE ceiling` (default sigma_max=5.0 at line 123/188)
verdict.evidence_v2: transformer/core/vfe_utils.py:710,716 `_sigma_max_sq = sigma_max * sigma_max` ... `eig_new = eig_new.clamp(min=eps, max=_sigma_max_sq)` with comment line 708 `sigma_max bounds the standard deviation; covariance eigenvalues are σ².` (default sigma_max=5.0 at line 642)
verdict.finding: The two functions share the exact affine-invariant algorithm structure (symmetrize, eigh of Sigma, whitened tangent R=Sigma^{-1/2}(step*dSigma)Sigma^{-1/2}, Frobenius trust-region clamp via trust_region/(R_norm+eps), exp via second eigh, sandwich by Sigma^{1/2}, final eigh + floor). But they are NOT byte-for-byte equivalent: the FINAL OUTPUT EIGENVALUE CEILING differs by a factor of sigma_max. V2 (vfe_utils.py:710) computes `_sigma_max_sq = sigma_max * sigma_max` and clamps the output spectrum to [eps, sigma_max^2] = [1e-6, 25.0] at the default, on the explicit theory (comment + code) that sigma_max bounds the standard deviation and covariance eigenvalues are sigma^2. V3 (retraction.py:168) clamps the output spectrum to [eps, sigma_max] = [1e-6, 5.0], on the reinterpretation that the eigenvalues ARE variances so there is "ONE ceiling." For ANY input covariance with a post-retraction eigenvalue in (5.0, 25.0], the two functions return materially different SPD matrices at identical default parameters — an unconditional forward divergence, not a rename. This is a deliberate semantic reinterpretation of the ceiling, so the relationship is "divergent" (not "refactored," which would be behavior-preserving). Secondary corroborating divergences, not the primary kill: (1) the registered wrapper retract_spd_affine defaults trust_region=5.0 (retraction.py:186) whereas V2 retract_spd_torch defaults trust_region=2.0 (vfe_utils.py:640) — so the claim's stated implication "V3's spd_affine default == V2's retract_spd_torch" fails at the wrapper layer too; note the bare cited V3 retract_spd_full itself defaults trust_region=2.0, matching V2. (2) Eigh helper differs: V3 _eigh_damped forward is bit-identical to torch.linalg.eigh (only the backward gradient is Lorentzian-regularized), whereas V2 _safe_eigh adds escalating diagonal jitter that can perturb the forward, though only on retry escalation. Any golden test asserting V3==V2 here must be masking the ceiling divergence by passing matched custom params or only exercising eigenvalues below 5.0.

verifiedHighPoints.topic: Full-cov eigenvalue CEILING: sigma_max vs sigma_max^2
verifiedHighPoints.v3: vfe3/geometry/retraction.py:168 eig_new.clamp(min=eps, max=sigma_max) with comment 'eigenvalues ARE variances: ONE ceiling' (same un-squared ceiling as the diagonal arm)
verifiedHighPoints.v2: transformer/core/vfe_utils.py:710,716 _sigma_max_sq = sigma_max*sigma_max; eig_new.clamp(min=eps, max=_sigma_max_sq) — full-cov ceiling is sigma_max^2, while the diagonal path (vfe_utils.py:784) clamps to sigma_max
verifiedHighPoints.implication: A REAL numerical divergence in the full-covariance retraction. V2 treats sigma_max as a std-dev (eigenvalues = variances bounded by sigma_max^2) and is internally inconsistent (diag clamps the variance to sigma_max, full clamps to sigma_max^2). V3 unifies both arms to clamp the variance/eigenvalue to sigma_max. At default sigma_max=5.0, V2's full-cov ceiling is 25 vs V3's 5 — different SPD trust regions; full-cov runs are NOT byte-identical between V2 and V3.

verdict.topic: Full-cov eigenvalue CEILING: sigma_max vs sigma_max^2
verdict.evidence_v3: vfe3/geometry/retraction.py:168 `eig_new = eig_new.clamp(min=eps, max=sigma_max)` (full-cov retract_spd_full); diagonal arm at line 112 `sigma_new.clamp(min=eps, max=sigma_max)` — both arms ceiling = sigma_max (default 5.0, line 188)
verdict.evidence_v2: transformer/core/vfe_utils.py:710 `_sigma_max_sq = sigma_max * sigma_max`, :716 `eig_new = eig_new.clamp(min=eps, max=_sigma_max_sq)` (full-cov retract_spd_torch, default sigma_max=5.0 at :642); diagonal arm at :787 `sigma_new.clamp(min=eps, max=sigma_max)` (default 5.0 at :734) — full ceiling = sigma_max^2 (25), diag ceiling = sigma_max (5)
verdict.finding: Claim is accurate; refutation fails. Direct reads confirm the asymmetry on both sides. V2's full-cov retraction clamps eigenvalues to sigma_max^2 (=25 at the default sigma_max=5.0) while V2's diagonal retraction clamps to sigma_max (=5) — internally inconsistent, exactly as claimed. V3 unifies both arms to sigma_max (=5). Defaults are identical (sigma_max=5.0) on both repos and in all four functions, so no hidden pre-squaring offsets it. I checked whether sigma_max might be passed in already-squared at the call site or whether V3 squares elsewhere: V3 never forms sigma_max^2 anywhere in retraction.py (grep shows only `max=sigma_max`). The single discrepancy I found is a cosmetic path typo in the claim — the V2 file is `transformer/core/vfe_utils.py`, not `transformer/vfe/transformer/core/vfe_utils.py` — but the cited line numbers and content match verbatim. Side note (does not affect verdict): V3's retract_logeuclidean_full docstring at :231 claims it projects to [eps, sigma_max^2], yet the code at :264 uses max=sigma_max — a comment-vs-code mismatch in V3, but code (ground truth) confirms V3 uses sigma_max. Conclusion: a genuine numerical divergence; full-covariance runs are not byte-identical between V2 and V3 whenever an output eigenvalue falls in (5, 25].

verifiedHighPoints.topic: Gauge-RoPE: true transport sandwich vs mu-only pre-KL rotation
verifiedHighPoints.v3: vfe3/geometry/rope.py:47-78 build_rope_rotation: BLOCK-DIAGONAL R(theta) within each irrep block (pairs theta=n*base^{-2k/d}, default base=100), applied as Omega^RoPE_ij = R_i Omega_ij R_j^T (transport.py:74-79,398-403); covariance rotated only when on_cov/rope_full_gauge (transport.py:438-443)
verifiedHighPoints.v2: transformer/core/transport_ops.py:115-178 _apply_rope rotates mu by its own absolute position (pairs across full K, theta=base^{-2k/K}, default base=10000) BEFORE the KL (non_flat.py:517-519), leaving Sigma raw; _apply_rope_to_covariance:181-234 is the separate experimental rope_full_gauge sandwich
verifiedHighPoints.implication: Materially different RoPE realizations. V3 folds the rotation into the transport OPERATOR as a genuine gauge sandwich R_i Omega_ij R_j^T (block-diagonal within irreps, preserving the block-exp fast path, base=100), so it is a coherent gauge action; V2 rotates only the scoring means pre-KL across the full K with base=10000 (the standard Q/K pattern, which V2's own docstring flags as neither the full gauge interp nor a textbook rotated-Gaussian KL). Both expose a full-gauge toggle (V3 on_cov/rope_full_gauge, V2 rope_full_gauge) that adds the Sigma sandwich. Different frequency basis (per-block d vs full K), different default base, and different operator placement mean RoPE outputs are NOT equivalent between the two.

verdict.topic: Gauge-RoPE: true transport sandwich vs mu-only pre-KL rotation
verdict.evidence_v3: vfe3/geometry/transport.py:401-403 "m = torch.einsum(\"...jlk,...jl->...jk\", omega.rope, mu) ... t = transport_mean(omega.base, m) ... return torch.einsum(\"...ikl,...ijl->...ijk\", omega.rope, t)  # post-rotate by R_i"; rope.py:71 "freq = base ** (-2.0 * k / d)" (per-block d) with rope.py:53 "base: float = 100.0"
verdict.evidence_v2: transformer/core/transport_ops.py:115 "def _apply_rope(mu: torch.Tensor, base: float = 10000.0)" with :154-155 "B, N, K = mu.shape; half_K = K // 2" and :79 "freqs = 1.0 / (base ** (torch.arange(0, half_K, ...) / half_K))" (full-K basis); :104 "positions = torch.arange(N, ...)" (absolute position); called in transformer/vfe/non_flat.py:519 "mu = _apply_rope(mu, base=rope_base)" BEFORE the KL loop, with un-rotated transport Omega_h at :527
verdict.finding: The divergent relationship holds. V2 path resolves at C:/Users/chris and christine/Desktop/VFE_2.0/transformer (the task's /vfe/ segment is wrong; actual file transformer/core/transport_ops.py and transformer/vfe/non_flat.py). I attacked the claim by hunting for a per-block/operator-folded RoPE variant in V2 and a full-K variant in V3; neither exists. Two HARD pillars survive: (1) OPERATOR PLACEMENT — V3 folds the rotation into the transport operator as R_i Omega_ij R_j^T (transport.py:401-403: pre-rotate key mean by R_j^T, transport on bare base, post-rotate result by R_i), whereas V2 rotates each mean once by its own ABSOLUTE position (transport_ops.py:104 arange(N)) BEFORE the KL (non_flat.py:519) and the transport Omega_h stays un-rotated (non_flat.py:527). Even with base/frequency equalized these are non-equivalent: R_i Omega_ij R_j^T mu_j (query left un-rotated) differs from V2's per-token absolute rotation of every mean. (2) FREQUENCY BASIS — V3 uses per-irrep-block d (rope.py:71 base**(-2k/d), looping start over irrep_dims at rope.py:68), V2 uses the full K (transport_ops.py:79 base**(arange(half_K)/half_K), half_K=K//2 at :155, NO irrep_dims split); these coincide only in the degenerate single-block (one irrep = K) case. One SOFT pillar partially collapses: the function-signature default base IS 100 in V3 (rope.py:53) vs 10000 in V2 (transport_ops.py:115), but a V2 call site already passes rope_base=100.0 (non_flat.py:480/519), so default-base is not a clean discriminator — the verdict does NOT depend on it. Both expose a full-gauge Sigma sandwich toggle (V3 on_cov/RopeTransport at transport.py:438-443; V2 _apply_rope_to_covariance at transport_ops.py:181-237 under rope_full_gauge), matching the claim. V3's default means-only path is itself flagged non-coherent (transport.py:62-66: "NOT a single coherent congruence image"), so the "coherent gauge action" descriptor applies to V3's on_cov=True path; the claim already balances this. Materially different RoPE realizations confirmed.

dimensions.dimension: Prior bank / encode / decode kernels (V3 vfe3/model/prior_bank.py vs V2 transformer/vfe/prior_bank.py)
dimensions.summary: V3 is a leaner reimplementation that keeps the core decode mechanism (diagonal KL-to-prior with a learnable, clamped softmax temperature) numerically equivalent to V2 up to softmax-invariant shifts, but drops V2's entire prior-construction richness. V2's DEFAULT prior is the gauge-fixed shared-base orbit (mu_v = A_v mu_0, Sigma_v = A_v diag(s) A_v^T) with phi det-control applied at encode; in V3 that orbit is a NotImplementedError stub and the only working encode is the direct per-token table lookup. V3 also drops the mixture decode (decode_n_components / learn_weights), drops the sigma upper cap (sigma_max), changes the sigma floor (0.01 -> 1e-6), and adds fp32-stability mean-centering plus a fused chunked CE memory win and a use_prior_bank-gated linear readout that V2 keeps in a different file. neither file implements a learnable per-vocab decode_bias.

points.topic: Fused diagonal KL-to-prior decode kernel (the core readout)
points.v3: _decode_diagonal builds lhs=[sigma_q+mc_q^2, -2 mc_q], rhs=[1/sigma_v, mc_v/sigma_v], a_v=lhs@rhs^T + sum(mc_v^2/sigma_v + log sigma_v); kl_v=0.5(a_v - per_pos); returns -kl_v/tau_eff (prior_bank.py:409-453)
points.v2: _diag_decode_logits builds lhs=[sigma_q+mu_q^2, -2 mu_q], rhs=[1/sigma_p, mu_p/sigma_p], combined=lhs@rhs^T; prior_bias=sum(mu_p^2/sigma_p + log sigma_p); returns -0.5*scale/tau*(combined+prior_bias) (prior_bank.py:895-919)
points.implication: Both reduce to the same closed-form diagonal KL via the identical 2K-wide matmul factorization. This is the load-bearing decode the golden tests must pin; the algebra is the same Gaussian-diagonal KL.

points.topic: Learnable decode temperature decode_tau / decode_log_scale
points.v3: _tau_eff = base_tau * exp(-clamp(decode_log_scale,-3,3)); decode returns -KL/tau_eff (prior_bank.py:215-221, 453). decode_log_scale init zeros(1) (prior_bank.py:132)
points.v2: scale=exp(clamp(decode_log_scale,-3,3)); logits = -0.5*scale/tau*(...). decode_log_scale init zeros(1) (prior_bank.py:304, 918-919)
points.implication: Algebraically identical: V3's -KL/(decode_tau*exp(-s)) == V2's -(exp(s)/decode_tau)*KL, same [-3,3] clamp, both init s=0 so untrained logits = -KL/decode_tau bitwise. Core mechanism matches.

points.topic: gauge_fixed_priors shared-base orbit prior (mu_v = A_v mu_0, Sigma_v = A_v diag(s) A_v^T)
points.v3: encode_mode='gauge_fixed' is a NAMED STUB that raises NotImplementedError; only 'per_token' direct lookup works (prior_bank.py:393-406). No base_mu/base_log_sigma, no _apply_gauge_transform, no fused_block_matrix_exp_pairs
points.v2: gauge_fixed_priors=True is the DEFAULT and fully implemented: base_mu/base_log_sigma (prior_bank.py:188-192), _apply_gauge_transform sandwiches per irrep block (prior_bank.py:412-493), encode calls it (prior_bank.py:608-610)
points.implication: Largest difference. V2's default prior lives on a gauge orbit of one shared base belief (capacity V*n_gen); V3 has no orbit prior at all and only supports direct per-token (mu,sigma) tables. V3's encode==V2's gauge_fixed_priors=False branch, never V2's default.

points.topic: Direct per-token prior lookup (encode q=p)
points.v3: _encode_per_token: mu=_prior_mu_table()[ids], sigma=exp(sigma_log)[ids].clamp(min=eps), phi=phi_embed[ids]; diag or diag_embed by diagonal_covariance (prior_bank.py:373-390)
points.v2: _per_token_priors + encode (gauge_fixed_priors=False branch): mu=mu_embed(ids), sigma=exp(log_sigma).clamp(eps,sigma_max); phi=phi_embed(ids); _diag_to_full_block when full-cov (prior_bank.py:359-375, 611-617)
points.implication: The direct-mode encode paths compute the same belief tuple. Differences are the clamp bounds (see sigma-floor point) and V3 storing tables as raw nn.Parameter vs V2 nn.Embedding (cosmetic).

points.topic: Sigma floor and upper cap (eps / sigma_max)
points.v3: eps=1e-6 used as the only floor at encode and decode; NO sigma_max anywhere (prior_bank.py:105, 387, 437, 451)
points.v2: eps=0.01 floor AND sigma_max upper cap on every sigma: encode clamp(eps,sigma_max) (prior_bank.py:373), decode clamp(min=eps) at 858/864 and clamp(eps,sigma_max) at mixture 888
points.implication: Numerically divergent: V2 floors variances at 0.01 and caps at sigma_max; V3 floors at 1e-6 and never caps. Breaks bitwise parity unless the golden-test config aligns floors; also when the E-step drives sigma small the two decode KLs differ.

points.topic: fp32 mean-centering before the decode matmul (c = mean_v(mu_v))
points.v3: Subtracts v-independent c=mu_v.mean(0) from both mu_q and mu_v before forming lhs/rhs (mc_q, mc_v) to kill catastrophic cancellation in the Mahalanobis reconstruction (prior_bank.py:441-448)
points.v2: No centering: lhs uses raw mu_q, rhs raw mu_p directly (prior_bank.py:913-917)
points.implication: Analytically identical ((mu_q-c)-(mu_v-c)==mu_q-mu_v) but fp32-divergent: V3 is more accurate at large |mu|/tight sigma. A direct float32 golden compare can disagree at the atol where V2's uncentered matmul cancels.

points.topic: Per-position v-invariant constant carried in raw logits
points.v3: Keeps the full -K - sum_k log sigma_q term (per_pos) so logits == exact -KL/tau_eff over the whole domain (prior_bank.py:451-453, docstring 426-427)
points.v2: Drops -K and log|Sigma_q| as softmax-invariant; logits are -KL/tau up to a per-row shift (prior_bank.py docstring 807, only prior_bias carried)
points.implication: Identical under softmax/cross-entropy (the dropped terms are per-position constants in v), but the RAW logit tensors differ by a per-position additive shift. A direct logits golden compare must account for this; a CE/argmax compare is unaffected.

points.topic: decode_n_components mixture decode + decode_mixture_learn_weights
points.v3: n/a - absent (grep: decode_n_components / decode_mixture_learn_weights have 0 hits in prior_bank.py)
points.v2: C1 mixture: extra (M-1) per-token (mu,log-sigma) component tables; decode = logsumexp_m(log w_{v,m} - KL/tau) via online logaddexp; optional learnable mixture_log_weights (V,M) (prior_bank.py:306-339, 867-893)
points.implication: V3 dropped the multi-component mixture decode entirely. V3 has only the single-Gaussian KL readout, so the ~M-fold rank lift past the single-Gaussian ceiling is not available in V3.

points.topic: exact_full_cov_decode / full-covariance decode kernel
points.v3: decode_mode='full' (_decode_full) scores the full (K,K) posterior against diagonal-embedded priors through the gaussian_full divergence SEAM (Cholesky KL), kl_max=inf, O(B*N*V*K^3) (prior_bank.py:473-496)
points.v2: _decode_exact_full_cov inlines per-irrep-BLOCK Cholesky (sum_h KL_h) with eigh SPD-projection of the block-dense prior Sigma_p, fused per-block matmul (prior_bank.py:644-775); gated by exact_full_cov_decode flag (prior_bank.py:841-842)
points.implication: Same goal (exact full-cov KL decode) but different structure: V2 sums per-block KLs on a block-DENSE gauge-orbit prior (A diag A^T) and hardens it via eigh-clamp; V3 routes the whole K-dim through one full-cov seam on a strictly DIAGONAL-embedded prior. Results differ whenever the prior carries within-block off-diagonal mass (which V3 priors never do).

points.topic: use_prior_bank-gated linear readout (logits = mu @ W^T ablation)
points.v3: _decode_linear + output_proj_weight (V,K) raw nn.Parameter, xavier-init, gated by use_prior_bank=False; decode() routes to 'linear' kernel (prior_bank.py:92, 141-145, 238-239, 499-515)
points.v2: n/a - absent in this file (grep: use_prior_bank / decode_mode / output_proj have 0 hits in prior_bank.py; V2's decode is unconditionally KL-to-prior). The V2-parity linear readout lives outside prior_bank.py
points.implication: Within the compared files this is V3-only: V3 pulled the VFE_2.0-parity linear output projection INTO the bank behind use_prior_bank. V2's prior_bank.py never decodes linearly, so the gating seam is a V3 structural addition.

points.topic: Fused chunked-vocab cross-entropy (decode_ce_diagonal_chunked / diagonal_chunked)
points.v3: decode_ce_diagonal_chunked iterates vocab in chunks, gradient-checkpoints each chunk's (B,N,Vc) logits, reduces to per-chunk logsumexp + target gather, never materializes (B,N,V); 'diagonal_chunked' inference kernel delegates to _decode_diagonal (prior_bank.py:272-370, 456-470)
points.v2: n/a - absent (no chunked-CE / checkpointed decode in prior_bank.py; V2 always forms the full (B,N,V) logits)
points.implication: V3-only training-memory optimization: the fused decode+CE keeps peak memory off (B,N,V) for large vocab. Value/grad match the full diagonal path exactly (deterministic recompute), so it is a pure efficiency add, not a behavioral change.

points.topic: Learnable per-vocab output decode_bias
points.v3: n/a - absent; V3 explicitly declines an output bias as a constant-in-V softmax-absorbed shift (prior_bank.py:139-140, grep decode_bias: 0 hits)
points.v2: n/a - absent; grep 'bias' returns only prior_bias (the KL prior-side term quad_p+log_det_p), NOT a learnable per-vocab output bias (prior_bank.py:761,917)
points.implication: The task premise that decode_bias is a V2-only feature is not borne out: neither file has a learnable per-vocab output bias. Both rely on softmax to absorb any constant-in-V shift. Documented as absent-both, not a difference.

points.topic: phi determinant control at encode (phi_project_slk / phi_trace_clamp)
points.v3: n/a - absent (grep phi_project_slk/phi_trace_clamp: 0 hits in prior_bank.py); phi_embed[ids] is returned raw in _encode_per_token (prior_bank.py:388)
points.v2: _apply_phi_det_control projects/clamps per-block trace directions of phi; applied inside encode before prior construction (prior_bank.py:558-590, 606-607)
points.implication: V2 regularizes the gauge frame's per-block determinant direction at encode; V3 returns phi untouched. Tied to V2's gauge-orbit prior machinery (which V3 lacks), so its absence is consistent with the dropped gauge_fixed path.

points.topic: reference_decode divergence-agnostic seam path
points.v3: reference_decode broadcasts the divergence.kl seam over V with kl_max=inf (general O(B*N*V*K), divergence-family-agnostic), used to pin the fused diagonal kernel (prior_bank.py:241-270)
points.v2: n/a - absent; V2 has no separate divergence-seam reference decode in prior_bank.py (decode is hard-coded diagonal/full-cov closed forms only)
points.implication: V3 adds a modular per-family reference decode used as the verification oracle for the fused kernel, reflecting V3's registry-driven divergence seam. Behaviorally it reproduces the diagonal KL; it is an added test/verification surface, not a runtime path change.

verifiedHighPoints.topic: Fused diagonal KL-to-prior decode kernel (the core readout)
verifiedHighPoints.v3: _decode_diagonal builds lhs=[sigma_q+mc_q^2, -2 mc_q], rhs=[1/sigma_v, mc_v/sigma_v], a_v=lhs@rhs^T + sum(mc_v^2/sigma_v + log sigma_v); kl_v=0.5(a_v - per_pos); returns -kl_v/tau_eff (prior_bank.py:409-453)
verifiedHighPoints.v2: _diag_decode_logits builds lhs=[sigma_q+mu_q^2, -2 mu_q], rhs=[1/sigma_p, mu_p/sigma_p], combined=lhs@rhs^T; prior_bias=sum(mu_p^2/sigma_p + log sigma_p); returns -0.5*scale/tau*(combined+prior_bias) (prior_bank.py:895-919)
verifiedHighPoints.implication: Both reduce to the same closed-form diagonal KL via the identical 2K-wide matmul factorization. This is the load-bearing decode the golden tests must pin; the algebra is the same Gaussian-diagonal KL.

verdict.topic: Fused diagonal KL-to-prior decode kernel (the core readout)
verdict.evidence_v3: vfe3/model/prior_bank.py:451-453 "per_pos = pb.K + torch.log(sigma_q.clamp(min=pb.eps)).sum(-1, keepdim=True) ... kl_v = 0.5 * (a_v - per_pos) ... return -kl_v / tau_eff"; centering at 441-443 "c = mu_v.mean(dim=0, keepdim=True) ... mc_v = mu_v - c ... mc_q = mu_q - c". Test tests/test_prior_bank.py:46 "assert torch.allclose(logits, ref, atol=1e-3)  # EXACT -KL/tau (per-position term kept)" pins against V3's OWN _reference_decode (line 9), not V2.
verdict.evidence_v2: prior_bank.py:919 "return -0.5 * scale / tau * (combined + prior_bias.unsqueeze(0).unsqueeze(0))" (NO -K, NO +sum log sigma_q term); docstring at 807 "Terms constant across V (``-K``, ``log|Sigma_q|``) are dropped (cancel in softmax)." No mu-centering: 913-914 feed raw "sigma_q_d + mu_q ** 2, -2.0 * mu_q" / "inv_sigma_p, mu_p_inv_sigma_p".
verdict.finding: The shared core (2K-wide fused matmul factorization lhs=[sigma_q+mu^2, -2mu], rhs=[1/sigma_v, mu_v/sigma_v]; prior_bias = sum(mu_v^2/sigma_v + log sigma_v); and the temperature fold of decode_log_scale clamped to [-3,3]: V2 scale/tau = exp(clamp(dls))/tau equals V3 1/tau_eff = exp(clamp(dls))/base_tau) is genuinely identical. But two real differences hide under "same": (1) FORMULA — V3 RETAINS the v-independent per-position term per_pos = K + sum_k log sigma_q (kl_v = 0.5(a_v - per_pos), returning exactly -KL/tau_eff), whereas V2 DROPS -K and log|Sigma_q| (returns only -0.5*scale/tau*(combined+prior_bias) = -KL/tau_eff plus a per-position v-independent constant). The raw logit tensors therefore are NOT equal; they differ by +0.5*(K + sum_k log sigma_q)/tau per (B,N) position. (2) NUMERICS — V3 subtracts c = mean_v(mu_v) from both means before the matmul (algebraically identity since (mu_q-c)-(mu_v-c)=mu_q-mu_v, but a float32 catastrophic-cancellation preconditioner V2 lacks); under large common mean offsets the two emit different float32 results. CONSUMED OUTPUT DOES NOT DIVERGE: softmax/cross-entropy/argmax are identical because the term-drop is v-independent (a per-row additive shift), so this is not a correctness regression. In-repo proof it is deliberate, not cosmetic: tests/test_prior_bank.py:46 asserts the EXACT raw -KL/tau pin ("per-position term kept") that the V2 formula would FAIL, alongside a separate shift-invariant log_softmax pin at :48 ("robust to a dropped-constant variant") that both forms pass. The golden pin is V3-against-its-own-reference, not V3-against-V2. Hence "refactored" (same KL algebra and same softmax output, different raw-logit formula + added centering), not "same"; and not "divergent" since the consumed output is identical.

verifiedHighPoints.topic: Learnable decode temperature decode_tau / decode_log_scale
verifiedHighPoints.v3: _tau_eff = base_tau * exp(-clamp(decode_log_scale,-3,3)); decode returns -KL/tau_eff (prior_bank.py:215-221, 453). decode_log_scale init zeros(1) (prior_bank.py:132)
verifiedHighPoints.v2: scale=exp(clamp(decode_log_scale,-3,3)); logits = -0.5*scale/tau*(...). decode_log_scale init zeros(1) (prior_bank.py:304, 918-919)
verifiedHighPoints.implication: Algebraically identical: V3's -KL/(decode_tau*exp(-s)) == V2's -(exp(s)/decode_tau)*KL, same [-3,3] clamp, both init s=0 so untrained logits = -KL/decode_tau bitwise. Core mechanism matches.

verdict.topic: Learnable decode temperature decode_tau / decode_log_scale
verdict.evidence_v3: vfe3/model/prior_bank.py:221 "return base_tau * torch.exp(-self.decode_log_scale.clamp(-3.0, 3.0))"; :453 "return -kl_v / tau_eff"; :132 "self.decode_log_scale = nn.Parameter(torch.zeros(1))"; :104 "decode_tau: float = 1.0"
verdict.evidence_v2: transformer/vfe/prior_bank.py:918 "scale = torch.exp(self.decode_log_scale.clamp(-3.0, 3.0))"; :919 "return -0.5 * scale / tau * (combined + prior_bias...)"; :304 "self.decode_log_scale = nn.Parameter(torch.zeros(1))"; :781 "tau: float = 1.0"
verdict.finding: The temperature mechanism is genuinely identical. V3: logits = -KL/tau_eff with tau_eff = decode_tau*exp(-clamp(s,-3,3)) => -KL*exp(s)/decode_tau. V2: logits = -(exp(clamp(s,-3,3))/tau)*0.5*(combined+prior_bias) => -KL*exp(s)/decode_tau. Same exp(+s) sharpening semantics, same [-3,3] clamp applied to s before exp, same s=0 zeros(1) init, same decode_tau=1.0 base. The denominator-temperature decode_tau*exp(-s) matches exactly. NOTE the actual V2 fused fn is _diag_decode_logits (lines 895-919); the name "_decode_single_component_diagonal" in the claim does not exist in the file, but the cited line numbers (304 init, 918-919 scale/return) are correct. One real but non-falsifying nuance: the claim says untrained logits match "bitwise." Raw logits do NOT match bitwise — V3's diagonal kernel KEEPS the per-position term -K + sum_k log sigma_q (prior_bank.py:451-452: per_pos = K + sum log sigma_q; kl_v = 0.5*(a_v - per_pos)), whereas V2 explicitly DROPS it (prior_bank.py:807: "Terms constant across V (-K, log|Sigma_q|) are dropped"). That term is constant across the vocabulary V, so it is a softmax/cross-entropy-invariant per-row shift: probabilities, argmax, and loss are identical, but the raw logit tensors differ by that per-position offset (itself scaled by exp(s)/tau). This difference lives in the KL-readout, not in the temperature parameter under test, so the temperature equivalence claim is intact; only the literal word "bitwise" overstates the raw-logit agreement (it should read "equal up to a softmax-invariant per-row constant").

verifiedHighPoints.topic: gauge_fixed_priors shared-base orbit prior (mu_v = A_v mu_0, Sigma_v = A_v diag(s) A_v^T)
verifiedHighPoints.v3: encode_mode='gauge_fixed' is a NAMED STUB that raises NotImplementedError; only 'per_token' direct lookup works (prior_bank.py:393-406). No base_mu/base_log_sigma, no _apply_gauge_transform, no fused_block_matrix_exp_pairs
verifiedHighPoints.v2: gauge_fixed_priors=True is the DEFAULT and fully implemented: base_mu/base_log_sigma (prior_bank.py:188-192), _apply_gauge_transform sandwiches per irrep block (prior_bank.py:412-493), encode calls it (prior_bank.py:608-610)
verifiedHighPoints.implication: Largest difference. V2's default prior lives on a gauge orbit of one shared base belief (capacity V*n_gen); V3 has no orbit prior at all and only supports direct per-token (mu,sigma) tables. V3's encode==V2's gauge_fixed_priors=False branch, never V2's default.

verdict.topic: gauge_fixed_priors shared-base orbit prior (mu_v = A_v mu_0, Sigma_v = A_v diag(s) A_v^T)
verdict.evidence_v3: vfe3/model/prior_bank.py:393-406 `@register_encode("gauge_fixed")` ... `raise NotImplementedError("encode_mode='gauge_fixed' is a named stub (gauge orbit from a shared base); use 'per_token'.")`. Grep over vfe3/ for `base_mu|_apply_gauge_transform|fused_block_matrix_exp|base_log_sigma|gauge_orbit`: "No matches found". Grep for `gauge_fixed_priors`: "No matches found". Only two registered encode kernels exist: `per_token` (working, lines 373-390) and `gauge_fixed` (stub).
verdict.evidence_v2: vfe/prior_bank.py:188-192 `if self.gauge_fixed_priors: ... self.base_mu = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(K) * cfg.mu_init_std); self.base_log_sigma = nn.Parameter(torch.full((K,), sigma_init_log))`; :412-416 `def _apply_gauge_transform(...)` docstring `mu_v = A_v \mu_0, \Sigma_v = A_v \Sigma_0 A_v^\top`; :443 `mu_parts.append(torch.einsum('...ij,j->...i', exp_h, base_mu_h))`; :450 `sigma_h = exp_h_f32 @ sigma_diag @ exp_h_f32.transpose(-2, -1)`; :608-610 `if self.gauge_fixed_priors: block_exp_pairs = self._compute_block_exp_pairs(phi); mu_p, sigma_p = self._apply_gauge_transform(block_exp_pairs)`.
verdict.finding: The relationship is genuinely divergent and survives the adversarial attack. I attempted to refute by hunting for any V3 gauge-orbit machinery under a renamed/relocated symbol: grep across the entire vfe3 package for base_mu, base_log_sigma, _apply_gauge_transform, fused_block_matrix_exp, gauge_orbit, and the config flag gauge_fixed_priors all returned ZERO matches. V3 has exactly one working encode kernel (per_token, direct (mu,sigma,phi) table lookup) and one NotImplementedError stub (gauge_fixed). V2 fully implements the orbit prior: base_mu (nn.Parameter) and base_log_sigma (nn.Parameter, exposed as base_sigma property at :350-356), the per-irrep-block sandwich A_h diag(s_h) A_h^T in _apply_gauge_transform (:412-493, both diagonal-approx and exact full-cov paths), and encode() dispatches to it on the gauge_fixed_priors=True branch (:608-610). So V3 has no orbit prior at all; V3's only encode == V2's gauge_fixed_priors=False per-token branch. The comparing agent's structural claim is correct. ONE refinement, not a refutation: calling V2 gauge-fixed "the DEFAULT" is only true of the in-class getattr fallback (prior_bank.py:186 defaults True); the VFEConfig dataclass field defaults False (config.py:448) and every actual training preset sets it False (presets.py:114, train_vfe.py:59, train_pure_fep.py:105, vfe_ablation_suite.py:120). This does not weaken the divergence — it reinforces it: V2's active runs use the per-token branch that V3 mirrors, while the orbit-prior capacity machinery EXISTS and is fully wired in V2 yet is entirely absent (stub-only) in V3.

dimensions.summary: V3 realizes the CLAUDE.md "config-selected registry behind every seam" principle with three first-class, decorator-populated registries — divergence functionals (_FUNCTIONALS via register_functional / get_functional), exponential families (_FAMILIES via register_family / get_family, a BeliefParams ABC with DiagonalGaussian/FullGaussian subclasses), and alpha self-coupling forms (_ALPHAS via register_alpha / get_alpha) — selected at call time by the config strings divergence_family, family, alpha_mode. V2 encodes the SAME three mathematical concepts as inline scalar/bool config flags (alpha_divergence float, diagonal_covariance bool, E_learnable_alpha bool) consumed directly by hardcoded free functions (diag_kl/diag_renyi in _numerics.py) and a method (get_bayesian_alpha) inside the monolithic E-step. The numerics of the central closed forms are deliberately the same (V3's gaussian.py ports the moment forms verbatim, and the diagonal-Renyi blend formula is structurally identical on both sides within the shared alpha∈(0,1] regime); the divergence is the architecture — registry seams that extend by registration vs inline flags that extend by editing call sites — plus a handful of genuine V3-only capabilities (a generic Bregman/Renyi-from-A fallback, a closed-form full-Gaussian family object, a squared_hellinger functional, a learnable/per-coord alpha registry) and one V3-only PD-masking robustness change on the alpha>1 branch.

points.topic: Architectural philosophy: divergence selection (registry vs inline flag)
points.v3: divergence.py:151-168 register_functional/get_functional/divergence_functionals registry; free_energy.py:89 `functional = get_functional(divergence_family)` selects at call time by the config STRING divergence_family (default 'renyi', families/base.py:281-282 registers 'renyi' and 'squared_hellinger')
points.v2: config.py:211 `alpha_divergence: float = 1.0` is a numeric flag; e_step.py:650-658 branches inline on `_renyi_aware_alpha()` between hardcoded diag_renyi vs an inlined KL formula. No functional registry exists; the divergence 'family' is a number, not a named, registered object
points.implication: This is the central architectural difference. In V3 a new divergence (e.g. squared_hellinger) slots in by writing-and-registering with zero call-site edits; in V2 every new divergence requires an inline branch at each consumer. Realizes CLAUDE.md 'config-selected registry behind every seam' that V2 only gestures at via flags.

points.topic: Diagonal Gaussian closed-form KL/Renyi numerics (the pinned core)
points.v3: families/gaussian.py:66-108 DiagonalGaussian.renyi_closed_form: KL branch 0.5*(trace + mahal - K + logdet) at lines 83-87; Renyi branch with blend (1-alpha)*sigma_q + alpha*sigma_t, mahal = alpha*delta^2/blend, logdet/(alpha-1) at lines 95-105
points.v2: _numerics.py:224-256 diag_kl 0.5*(sq/sp + delta^2/sp - 1 + log sp - log sq) (per-dim, caller sums to -K); _numerics.py:259-296 diag_renyi same blend sigma_blend=(1-alpha)*sq+alpha*sp, mahal=alpha*delta^2/blend, logdet/(alpha-1)
points.implication: The mathematically load-bearing closed forms are byte-identical in the pure alpha∈(0,1] regime (V3 docstring says 'ported verbatim'). This is exactly the equivalence the golden tests must pin; it must match V2 for parity, and structurally it does. Difference is packaging (method on a parameter object vs free function), not math.

points.topic: Family abstraction: parameter-object ABC vs covariance bool
points.v3: families/base.py:48-117 BeliefParams ABC (natural/log_partition_at/entropy/expected_statistic/block/stack); gaussian.py:19 @register_family('gaussian_diagonal') DiagonalGaussian, gaussian.py:149 @register_family('gaussian_full') FullGaussian; cov_kind ClassVar is 'the single source of truth' (base.py:67, replacing name sniffing)
points.v2: config.py:350 `diagonal_covariance: bool = True` ('True=(B,N,K), False=(B,N,K,K)'); e_step.py:382 self.diagonal_covariance read; divergences are dimension-sniffed at runtime (e_step.py:636 `if sigma_q.dim()==4: torch.diagonal(...)`), not dispatched on a family object
points.implication: V3 makes the exponential family a registered first-class object so a new family (e.g. a non-Gaussian member) slots in behind the seam by subclassing+registering; V2's family is a single boolean toggle with shape-sniffing scattered across call sites. The cov_kind ClassVar removes V2's fragile dim()==4 / name-sniffing dispatch.

points.topic: Generic Bregman / Renyi-from-A divergence fallback
points.v3: families/base.py:171-211 _renyi_from_log_partition: for a family lacking renyi_closed_form, computes KL=A(tp)-A(tq)-<gradA,tp-tq> (alpha=1) and R=(A(blend)-alpha A(tq)-(1-alpha)A(tp))/(alpha-1) (alpha!=1) from log_partition_at + expected_statistic; renyi() dispatches via getattr(q,'renyi_closed_form',None) at divergence.py:236-239
points.v2: n/a - absent
points.implication: V3 gives ANY exponential family a working divergence for free from just A(theta) and gradA, so a new family need not hand-derive a closed form. V2 has no such generality — every divergence is a hand-coded Gaussian formula. The Jun-2 observation flags that this reducer's per-axis contraction (base.py:201-204) was a fixed latent bug, so it is exercised, not vestigial.

points.topic: Full-covariance Gaussian divergence: closed-form family vs not-wired autograd
points.v3: families/gaussian.py:204-271 FullGaussian.renyi_closed_form: full Cholesky-based KL (trace/mahal/logdet via safe_cholesky, lines 231-243) AND alpha!=1 blend with per-element PD masking (lines 252-270); natural/log_partition_at (178-193) enable the generic path too
points.v2: diag_renyi/diag_kl are diagonal-only (_numerics.py:224,259); the full-cov self-KL via the autograd path is explicitly NotImplementedError at e_step.py:2544-2552 ('full-cov self-KL via autograd needs logdet through eigendecomposition, not yet wired')
points.implication: V3 has a genuine, complete closed-form full-Gaussian divergence as a registered family member; the equivalent V2 path raises NotImplementedError. This is a real capability V3 adds, not a refactor — V2's full-cov support is partial and routed through other (non-divergence-primitive) machinery.

points.topic: Squared-Hellinger divergence functional
points.v3: divergence.py / families/base.py:254-278 squared_hellinger: H^2 = 1 - exp(-D_{1/2}/2) built on renyi(alpha=0.5), registered as a second functional (base.py:282)
points.v2: n/a - absent (config.py:211 only documents alpha=0.5 as 'Bhattacharyya', but it stays a Renyi-alpha value; no separate Hellinger functional or H^2 transform exists)
points.implication: Concrete proof the V3 functional seam pays off: a second divergence family ships as ~25 lines + one register_functional line, no consumer edits. The same in V2 would require threading a new flag and inline branches through every divergence call site.

points.topic: Alpha self-coupling: registry of forms vs E_learnable_alpha bool
points.v3: alpha_i.py:19-46 register_alpha/get_alpha/alpha_is_per_coord registry; forms 'constant' (60-69), 'state_dependent' (72-84), 'learnable' (87-113), 'state_dependent_per_coord' (116-135); dispatched by config alpha_mode via self_coupling_alpha(...,mode=...) at alpha_i.py:166-181
points.v2: config.py:325 `E_learnable_alpha: bool = True`; e_step.py:626-627 `if not self.E_learnable_alpha: return self.alpha` (constant) else c0/(b0+div_k) at e_step.py:660. Exactly two states (constant scalar vs Bayesian per-dim), selected by one bool inside a method
points.implication: V3 turns V2's two-valued bool into an open registry with four registered schedules and a per_coord routing flag, extensible by registration. The 'constant' and 'state_dependent' forms reproduce V2's two branches (c0/(b0+D) matches e_step.py:660), so the existing behavior is preserved while the seam generalizes.

points.topic: State-dependent alpha stationary form alpha* = c0/(b0+D) and its regularizer
points.v3: alpha_i.py:82-84 alpha = c0/(b0+kl).clamp(min=eps); alpha_i.py:49-57 alpha_regularizer R = b0*alpha - c0*log(alpha)
points.v2: e_step.py:660 `return c0/(b0 + div_k)`; e_step.py:51-52 docstring R(alpha_i)=b0*alpha_i - c0*log alpha_i; the same Gamma-Normal conjugacy schedule
points.implication: The adaptive precision schedule and its log-barrier regularizer are mathematically identical on both sides; V3 just factors them into a registered form + an explicit alpha_regularizer helper. The envelope cancellation (alpha_i.py:138-163 alpha_gradient_coefficient) makes V2's product-rule correction a structural identity rather than a hand-maintained copy.

points.topic: Learnable scalar alpha = exp(log_alpha) (NN-exception coupling)
points.v3: alpha_i.py:87-113 @register_alpha('learnable'): alpha=exp(log_alpha) from a model nn.Parameter, zero regularizer, log_alpha=0 => alpha=1 reproduces constant at init (sanctioned NN exception, default off)
points.v2: n/a - absent (V2's only 'learnable' alpha is the Bayesian raw_c0/raw_b0 nn.Parameters at e_step.py:315-316 driving c0/(b0+D); there is no free exp(log_alpha) scalar coupling)
points.implication: Different notion of 'learnable': V2 learns the Gamma hyperparameters c0,b0 (precision stays a posterior summary); V3 adds, as a separate registry member, a FREE scalar coupling with no Gamma prior and zero R — a distinct training behavior (plain alpha*D self-term, no envelope cancellation). Not a rename of V2's E_learnable_alpha.

points.topic: Per-coordinate alpha and divergence-shape routing
points.v3: alpha_i.py:116-135 @register_alpha('state_dependent_per_coord', per_coord=True) consumes UNSUMMED (...,N,K) D^(k); free_energy.py:175-200 self_divergence_for_alpha reads alpha_is_per_coord(mode) to route per-coord vs summed divergence; gaussian.py:110-146 renyi_per_coord supplies the unsummed terms
points.v2: n/a - absent (get_bayesian_alpha at e_step.py:660 always returns the per-dim c0/(b0+div_k) but there is no registry flag routing summed-vs-per-coord; the schedule is hardwired per-dim with no alternative)
points.implication: V3 adds a routing seam (per_coord flag) so an alpha form's divergence-reduction need is honoured by registration alone, with the per-coord path enforced to diagonal+renyi only. V2 has no such routing abstraction; its per-dim schedule is the only option and cannot be swapped for a per-position-summed coupling without code edits.

points.topic: alpha>1 (non-convex-blend) handling: PD masking vs config prohibition
points.v3: gaussian.py:95-108: alpha>1 blend coordinates with raw_blend<=0 are masked to NaN -> safe_kl_clamp -> kl_max (lines 106-107); a RuntimeWarning fires (base.py:30-38 _warn_alpha_gt_one, raised in renyi at divergence.py:234-235). alpha>1 is REACHABLE (only warned)
points.v2: diag_renyi (_numerics.py:289) clamps the blend to eps and returns the raw value with NO masking; instead config.py:1247-1254 __post_init__ HARD-RAISES ValueError for alpha_divergence outside (0,1], so V2 never executes the non-PD-blend path at all
points.implication: Within the pure alpha∈(0,1] regime the two are byte-identical (blend always SPD, mask inert per gaussian.py:90 comment). They diverge only OUTSIDE that regime: V2 forbids it at config build; V3 permits it with element-wise NaN->kl_max gating. A consumer running alpha>1 gets a hard error in V2 but a (warned) finite/clamped result in V3 — a genuine behavioral difference at the boundary.

points.topic: KL = Renyi(alpha=1) unification vs two separate functions
points.v3: divergence.py:242-251 kl(q,p) := renyi(q,p,alpha=1.0); renyi singularity at alpha=1 is dodged by an explicit `if abs(alpha-1)<1e-6` KL branch inside each closed form (gaussian.py:82, base.py:191)
points.v2: Two distinct free functions: diag_kl (_numerics.py:224) and diag_renyi (_numerics.py:259); callers must guard (e_step.py:650 `if self._renyi_aware_alpha()` picks diag_renyi else the inlined KL) because diag_renyi is singular at alpha=1 (_numerics.py:279 docstring)
points.implication: Same math, cleaner V3 surface: KL is a thin alpha=1 wrapper over the single renyi entry point, and the alpha=1 special-case lives once inside each family rather than as a caller-side guard duplicated at every V2 call site. Reduces the chance of a caller picking the wrong (singular) branch.

points.topic: safe_kl_clamp bounding of the divergence output
points.v3: families/base.py:19-27 safe_kl_clamp clamps to [0,kl_max] and maps NaN/+inf->kl_max, -inf->0; applied at the end of every closed form (gaussian.py:108,146,271) and the generic path (base.py:211)
points.v2: Equivalent safe_kl_clamp referenced in config.py:1242-1244 ('floored to 0 by safe_kl_clamp'); V2 diag_kl/diag_renyi themselves return UNCLAMPED per-dim values (_numerics.py:285 'unclamped, mirroring diag_kl'), the clamp lives in the consuming kernel
points.implication: Same clamping concept on both sides; placement differs. V3 bakes the clamp into the divergence primitive (so every functional output is bounded), whereas V2's per-dim primitives are unclamped and rely on the caller to clamp. Behaviorally equivalent for summed outputs but V3's per-primitive clamp is more defensive.

verifiedHighPoints.topic: Architectural philosophy: divergence selection (registry vs inline flag)
verifiedHighPoints.v3: divergence.py:151-168 register_functional/get_functional/divergence_functionals registry; free_energy.py:89 `functional = get_functional(divergence_family)` selects at call time by the config STRING divergence_family (default 'renyi', families/base.py:281-282 registers 'renyi' and 'squared_hellinger')
verifiedHighPoints.v2: config.py:211 `alpha_divergence: float = 1.0` is a numeric flag; e_step.py:650-658 branches inline on `_renyi_aware_alpha()` between hardcoded diag_renyi vs an inlined KL formula. No functional registry exists; the divergence 'family' is a number, not a named, registered object
verifiedHighPoints.implication: This is the central architectural difference. In V3 a new divergence (e.g. squared_hellinger) slots in by writing-and-registering with zero call-site edits; in V2 every new divergence requires an inline branch at each consumer. Realizes CLAUDE.md 'config-selected registry behind every seam' that V2 only gestures at via flags.

verdict.topic: Architectural philosophy: divergence selection (registry vs inline flag)
verdict.evidence_v3: vfe3/families/base.py:151 `def register_functional(name: str)`, :159 `def get_functional(name: str)`, :166 `def divergence_functionals()`, :281 `register_functional("renyi")(renyi)`, :282 `register_functional("squared_hellinger")(squared_hellinger)`; vfe3/free_energy.py:89 `functional = get_functional(divergence_family)`; vfe3/config.py:46 `divergence_family: str = "renyi"` validated :310 `_require(self.divergence_family, divergence_functionals(), "divergence_family")`
verdict.evidence_v2: config.py:211 `alpha_divergence: float    = 1.0     # Rényi α (1.0=KL, 0.5=Bhattacharyya)`; e_step.py:650-658 `if self._renyi_aware_alpha(): div_k = _diag_renyi(...)` else inline `trace_k = sigma_q_safe / sigma_p_safe ... div_k = 0.5 * (trace_k + mahal_k - 1 + logdet_k)`; grep over VFE_2.0/transformer/vfe for register_functional|get_functional|divergence_functionals|_FUNCTIONALS|divergence_family returns "No files found"
verdict.finding: The relationship survives the adversarial attack: it is a genuine architectural difference, not equivalence. V3 has a real string-keyed functional registry (register_functional/get_functional/divergence_functionals over a _FUNCTIONALS dict), selected at call time by the config STRING divergence_family (default "renyi", validated against the registry), with two members registered (renyi, squared_hellinger) and zero call-site edits needed to add a third. V2 has no such registry anywhere — a repo-wide grep for register_functional/get_functional/divergence_functionals/_FUNCTIONALS/divergence_family returns nothing — and selects divergence via the numeric alpha_divergence flag plus an inline boolean if/else (_renyi_aware_alpha gating _diag_renyi vs an inlined KL formula) at the consumer. One citation defect, NOT a substance error: the comparing agent cited the registry as divergence.py:151-168, but in the current tree divergence.py is a 45-line re-export shim and the actual definitions sit at families/base.py:151-168 (re-exported through divergence.py at lines 17-29). The line numbers 151-168 and the symbol names are exactly correct for families/base.py, so this is a misattributed file path, not a misread — the V3 mechanism, the V2 absence, and the implication (CLAUDE.md 'config-selected registry behind every seam') all hold.

verifiedHighPoints.topic: Diagonal Gaussian closed-form KL/Renyi numerics (the pinned core)
verifiedHighPoints.v3: families/gaussian.py:66-108 DiagonalGaussian.renyi_closed_form: KL branch 0.5*(trace + mahal - K + logdet) at lines 83-87; Renyi branch with blend (1-alpha)*sigma_q + alpha*sigma_t, mahal = alpha*delta^2/blend, logdet/(alpha-1) at lines 95-105
verifiedHighPoints.v2: _numerics.py:224-256 diag_kl 0.5*(sq/sp + delta^2/sp - 1 + log sp - log sq) (per-dim, caller sums to -K); _numerics.py:259-296 diag_renyi same blend sigma_blend=(1-alpha)*sq+alpha*sp, mahal=alpha*delta^2/blend, logdet/(alpha-1)
verifiedHighPoints.implication: The mathematically load-bearing closed forms are byte-identical in the pure alpha∈(0,1] regime (V3 docstring says 'ported verbatim'). This is exactly the equivalence the golden tests must pin; it must match V2 for parity, and structurally it does. Difference is packaging (method on a parameter object vs free function), not math.

verdict.topic: Diagonal Gaussian closed-form KL/Renyi numerics (the pinned core)
